bbarasiyargge mattavāṟu manuṣya brāhmaṇabhōjanada phalaṁ vikramādityarasara mada-
dācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ naḍayisuvarī dharmmamaṁ devasakke • • yyalu tiṁgaḷigo
l.note: The inscription stops here.

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 507 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE KALKANTHARAYA TEMPLE AT KALKANTHAPURA,
A HAMLET OF PEDDATUMBALAM, ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and mutilated. It refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya
king Pratāpachakravarti Jagadēkamalladēva (II) ruling from Kalyāṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE
CHENNAKESVARA TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and not dated. It refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya
king Jagadēkamalladēva, probably the second of that name, and mentions his feuda-
tory, • • • Pāṇḍyadēva ruling over Noḷambavāḍi thirtytwo-thousand, the
learned and warlike Mahājanas of Bāguḷi and a certain Vatsa-chamūpa. It registers
the grant of six honnus to the reader of the Purāṇas and four honnus to the reciter of
the Aindra hymns out of the interest of gadyāṇas 50.

l.ka:
jayatyāviṣkritaṁ viṣṇōrvvārāhaṁ kṣōbhitārṇnavaṁ dakṣiṇōnnatadaṁṣṭrāgraviśrāṁ-
tabhuvanaṁ vapuḥ || śubhōnnatalasatukīrttiśauryyasāmartthyaśālinaṁ

vatsadaṇḍādhipaṁ rakṣa vīrakēśava nityaśaḥ || śrīvīrakēśavadēvara purāṇavṛttigaṁ ·ayiṁdrakkaṁ
poṁge ·ippaṇa vriddhiyalu

kramavāhivāgi kaḍaṁgoṭṭa gadyāṇa 50 lekkadoḷaṁ gadyāṇa 50 ·adaṟa vriddhiyoḷage purā-
ṇakke ·āṟu honnaṁ kuḍuvaru ·ayiṁdrakke nālku

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1918.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING NEAR THE ISVARA TEMPLE AT GUNDAGATTI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and not dated. It refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king
Jagadēkamalladēva and mentions his feudatory, the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Pāṇḍya-
dēva as ruling over Noḷambavāḍi thirty-twothousand. On palaeographic grounds this
may be assigned to the reign of Jagadēkamalla II.
1. Ten more lines are completely damaged.

l.note: The inscription is broken after this.

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SUPPORTING THE BEAM AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE RANGANATHASVAMI
TEMPLE AT KORREVU, MADAKSIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a little damaged and not dated. It refers itself to the reign of the
Chāḷukya king Jagadēkamalladēva and mentions his feudatory Bammadēva-Chōḷa-
mahārāja as ruling over Gōvindavāḍi. The latter is stated to have made a gift of some
taxes for a perpetual lamp of the god Kammaṭēśvara. On palaeographic grounds
this may be relegated to the reign of Jagadēkamalla II.

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1920.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN A FIELD TO THE EAST OF THE VILLAGE UBACHERLA,
GOOTY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the 4th year of the Chāḷukya king Jagadēkamalla,
[Du]rmati, the other details being effaced. Siṅgi-[Nāyaka] and other Nāyakas are stated
to have contributed a portion of the toll-revenue for the service of the god Rāmēśvara
in addition to the grant of a village. Paleographically, the record may be assigned to
the reign of Jagadēkamalla II. In that case it should be read before No. 238.

l.note: Two lines on this face are damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 316 of 1925.)
ON A SLAB PLANTED BEFORE THE TEMPLE OF HANUMAN AT HALYAM,
KUDLIGI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1085 (mistake for 1075), Śrīmukha, Pushya, amāvāsye,
Sōmavāra, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, Sūrya-grahaṇa, Vyatīpāta. The date is irregular.
In Śrīmukha, Pushya-amāvāsyā corresponded to A.D. 1154 January 15 Friday, not
Monday, nor was there a solar eclipse on the day. It was not Uttarāyaṇa day,
too. The record refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king Tribhuvanamalla-
dēva and gives the genealogy of the dynasty from Tailapa to Vikramāditya VI.
It records the grant of some land for the service of the god Agastēśvara at Yeḷeha,
situated to the north of Koṭūr, by the king's feudatory Nāchidēvarasa of the
Kadamba family, who was ruling over Kōgaḷi five-hundred and Koṭūru twelve from
his capital, Koṭūru. The teacher Hēmarāśidēva and Būpajīya are said to have been
placed in charge of the lands granted to the same god by Bommidēvarasa and others.
Sōyidēvarasa, son of Bommidēva of Koṭūru, Chavuḍiseṭṭi, Māriseṭṭi and others are
also said to have granted lands for the service of the same god. Though the record
refers itself to the reign of Vikramāditya VI, its date falls in the reign of
Trailōkyamalla Taila III. The grant was probably put on stone in the year cited.

l.ka: stēśvaradēvanī jagatsvāmi niraṁtaraṁ bhaktakōṭige saṁtatamiṣṭasiddhiyaṁ || svasti samasta-
bhuvanāśrayaṁ śrīprithvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēsvara paramabhaṭṭāraka satyā-

thāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttamire taduvaṁśāvatārameṁteṁdaḍe || ·āṁta ripubhūmipara karaṁ tavi
si caḷu-
kyarājyamuditōditamappaṁtire nijabhujadiṁdeḷeyaṁ tānaseye tailapaṁ kṣitipāḷaṁ || vritta ||
naranāthāgraṇi tailapaṁge taneyaṁ satyāśrayaṁ tatsutaṁ dharaṇīvallabhavikramaṁ tadanujaṁ tāna-

yyaṇayyakṣitīsvaranātaṁganujaṁ jitāri jayasiṁhaṁ tattanūjaṁ nṛpēśvaranantāha[va*]ma-
llanātana

magaṁ sōmēśvarōrbīśvaraṁ || negaḷdirddā bhuvanaikamallananujaṁ cāḷukyanārāyaṇaṁ
nṛgamāṁ-
nōḷpī nāḷoḍeyanaṁ sāhityasarbajñanaṁ || vāridhimēkhaḷāvanige raṁjipa kuṁtaḷadē-
lentu nōrppaḍaṁ || ·ā puraduttarabhāgadoḷāpaḍiyane negaḷda yeḷehayeṁbudu lakṣmīpa-
hāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ nācidēvarasaru biṭṭa dharmmameṁteṁdeḍe śrīmanagastēśvaradēvara trikā-
narāhāradānakkaṁ dēvariṁ mūḍalu ·aḍakedōṁṭa kaṁba 200 dēvariṁ baḍagaṇa haḷḷiya dāri-

yiṁ paḍuvalu yerematta 1 haḷiya dāriyiṁ paḍuvalu matta 3 ·ā keyiṁ baḍagalu matta
1 dāriyiṁ

mūḍalu matta 1 cikakoṭura holavēreyiṁ keṁgāḍu matta 2 saṁkharadēvara dāriyiṁ gau-
ṭarageya mū-

ḍalu keṁgāḍu vāṭanakeṟeyalli gadde muttada kaḍahu kaṁba 51 paḍuvaṇa cāvikallali gadde
kaṁba 150 ·ūroḷa-

gaṇa nivēṣaṇa 4 gaṇavari so 1 koḍakke so 1 tōṭadali ·aḍakeya mara 1 yelevaḷi 1 dēva-
goḷaga dharmagoḷaga ·a-
yadāya gaṭṭigauḍa mādagauḍayaṁtivaru biṭṭa dharmmavaṁ pratipāḷisuvaru yācāryyaru
heṁmarāsidēvaru būpajīyaruṁ śrīyagastēśvaradēvarige maṁḍaḷika jagadaḷa bomidēvarasa-

ru koṭa keyi haḷḷiya dāriyiṁ muḍalu matta 1 || ·umādēviya pradhana paṁḍitayya gōpa-
rasa biṭṭa

keyi huḷiyare matta 1 modalēriya ·āladagade kaṁba 50 dēvara sutalu puragaṇa yiṁtī
biṭṭa dharmmavaṁ

pratipāḷisuvaru || koṭura hariharātmanu neṭṭane kaliboṁmidēvanātana kuvaranu paṭṭābhi-
rāmane-

nisida vairigharaṭṭaṁ jagadāḷa sōyidēvanripāḷaṁ || svasti śrīmānumāhāpradhānaṁ sarvvādhi-
kāri
ru || rākṣasasaṁvatsaradaṁdu (||) svasti śrīmatu sāmaṁtasamudra mallarāyakuvara nā-
śrīmadagastēsvaradēvarige tīrththadiṁ muḍali vōṇiyiṁ baḍagali koṭa ·eledōṁṭa • • •

śautra sarvvamā(ṁ)nya yiṁtī biṭṭa dharmmavaṁ pratipāḷakaru ·anaṁtagāvuḍa gōpāḷa-
[gauḍa]

vajīya būpajīyana moṁmakkaḷu lakkajīya nāgajīya || yī dharmma ·aḷipidaṁge vāraṇā-
siya[lu] kuru • • • •
brāmhaṇara koṁda pāpa || mallikārjunali tapōdhanara koṁda pāpa nāyakanaraka [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 517 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE PETE-BASAPPA TEMPLE AT CHINNATUMBALAM,
ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Yuva, Pau[sha], śu. 5, Sōmavāra, Uttarāyaṇa in the
reign of the Chāḷukya king Jagadēkamalladēva (II), corresponding, probably, to A.D.
1155 November 30 Wednesday (not Monday). The Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Āhavamalladēva
is stated to have made a gift of land for the service of the god Nakarēśvara set up by
Harihar-Āṇḍar of Vasishṭha-gōtra. It is to be noted that though. the record refers
itself to the reign of Jagadēkamalla (II) its date falls in the reign of his successor
Taila III who began to rule in A.D. 1150. (See No. 267 below).

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RAMESVARA AT YERRAGUDI,
KALYANADRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is much damaged and dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 80, Dhātṛi, Chaitra, śu.
10, corresponding to A.D. 1156 April 2. The rest is lost. The date is not verifiable.

l.note: The continuation is

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
OF THE SURYANARAYANA TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI
TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1082, Vikrama, Bhādrapada, puṇṇame,
Bṛihaspativāra, Sōma-grahaṇa, corresponding to A.D. 1160 August 18 Thursday,
when there was a lunar eclipse. It refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king
Jagadēkamalladēva and mentions his feudatory Vīra-Pāṇḍyadēva as ruling over
Kaḍambaḷige thousand, Ballakunda three-hundred and Kōgaḷi five-hundred. In the
presence of the fifty (Mahājanas) of Bāguḷi, Dharmavve, wife of the Brāhman general
Chiddaṇa, who was the son of the brave general Māḷapayya, and the daughter of
Padmanābha, who was the son of the Brāhman Dharmeya-Shaḍaṅgi of Kāśyapa-
gōtra, is said to have made a grant of land for the worship of the god Lakshmī-
nārāyaṇadēva set up by herself in Bāguḷi, for the pay of the śāstra teacher and of a
Purāṇa reader and for the subsistence of the priests employed in the temple.
Dharmavve is praised for her learning and character. Here it may be noted that
the record refers itself to the reign of Jagadēkamalla II, though it is known that
his successor began to rule in A.D. 1150.

l.ka:
daṁ vividhavidyādhīśvaraṁ viṣṇu lakṣmīnārāyaṇadēvanīge sukhamaṁ caṁdrārkkaruḷḷaṁnega ||

pāṁḍya nīṁ taguḷe matpriyaraṁ

neṟe kādudī triṇaṁ varanunivrittiliṁgavuragāleyavapucayaveṁdu pūjiparnneradu kaṟuṁke-
yeṁdu jinaneṁdu gajājinadhā-

riyeṁdu nāgaraposateṁdu jakkiyarēvenuttavanuddhatavairikāṁteyaru || va || svasti samadhi-

mārttaṇḍaṁ paricchēdiga(ṁ)ṇḍaṁ rājigacō-

ṟumaṁ kōgaḷiyaynūṟumaṁ sukhasaṁkathāvinōda-

dinabjaṣaṇḍadiṁ beḷadeṟagirdda keyvoladinunnatacaṁpakacōca-

nalva haṁse narttanamanoḍarccutirppa navilōvōtoḍagūḍada jakkavakki sanmanasija-
rāgadiṁ nereva daṁpati divyavanāṁtarāḷadoḷ || cārutarapravāḷaruci miṁcu

phaḷaṁgaḷaḷu sōneye sōneyenalu nirutaṁ basaṁtamuṁ

ki kabarībhare pallavahaste kīrakōkiḷakaḷanāde gucchanibiḍastani tānanisirppa tatpurīlalane-

mahītaḷāgradoḷ || śrīlalanānvi-

yde meynavirnnālageyādaḍaṁ pogaḷalārkume vāsugiyayvadiṁbara || bharatavyākaraṇā-

tamāge māḍidaṁ varavibhavānvitaṁ budhaśikhāmaṇi sudguṇavārddhi ciddaṇaṁ || satiratnaṁ cidda-

dhātri • • nayavinayasadācāracāritraśōbhānviteyaṁ sa-

ddharmeyaṁ dharmmaveyanakhiḷabhūmaṁḍaḷaṁ rāgadiṁdaṁ || ·ā dharmmaveya vaṁśāvatārameṁteṁ-
daḍe || kanakagirīndranaṁte vibudhāśrayaniṁdrana daṁtiyaṁtesadanu-

pamadāni cakradharanaṁte vinītanenalsamastasajjanatati kāśyapānvayaśirōmaṇiyaṁ dvijavaṁśa-
dīpanaṁ vinayadinēṁdudharmmeyaṣaḍaṁgiyanu-

dghaguṇaprasaṁgiya || kaṁ || ·ā vibhuvina sati mādave bhūviśrutaviśadakīrttiyantavara magaṁ
kōvidananantavāgvidyāvidananavadyavibhavasadmaṁ padmaṁ ||

vinayanidhi padmanābhana vanite mahīvinute bhuvanajīvanatanupāvane puṇyamūrtti nirmmaḷeya-
naghe pratyakṣagaṁge gaṁgādēvi

niravadyavaśiṣṭhānvayamuraripupathajāte vonalōcane phēnāṁbare haṁsayāne guṇamaṇi sari-
dadhipatipadmapatni gaṁgādēvi ||

girirājamēnakībhāsurajaṭharadoḷadrijāte puṭṭidavolu padmarasaṁgaṁ gaṁgavegaṁ dharaṇīsute dha-
rmmavaniteyudayaṁgeydaḷ ||

dhareyoḷage puruṣaratnaṁ varadaṁ śrīdharanudāttaramaṇīratnaṁ nirutaṁ dharmmaveyeṁdī hari-
māḍidanalte ratnamēḷāpakamaṁ ||

manumārggacaritre tavarmmaneyoḷu dharmmāṁganābhidhānadinātmēśanenippa ciddarājana mana-
yoḷu nāgāṁbikābhidhānadinesevaḷu || v® ||

sulalitadibyakaḷpalateyiṁ vanavāḍuva rājahaṁseyiṁ koḷanamṛtāṁśulēkheyiniruḷdinapaprabhe-
yiṁ nabhaṁ mahīvaḷayado-

ḷoppuvaṁte guṇi dharmmaveyiṁ varakāśyapānvayaṁ taḷedudu permmeyaṁ pesaranuṁnatiyaṁ
yaśamaṁ prabhutvamaṁ || hariyuviṁdire-

yuṁ sarōjatanūjanuṁ varavāṇiyuṁ girīśanuṁ girirājaputriyuviṁdranuṁ śaciyuṁ vaśi-
ṣṭharuvaruṁdhatiyuṁ viḷāsadinippavōlguṇa-

vārddhi ciddarasanuṁ vadhu dharmiyakkanuvirkke rāgadinurvviyoḷu || svasti yamaniyama-
spādhyāyadhyānadhāraṇamōnānuṣṭhānajapahō-

namayvadiṁbara saṁnidhānadalu śrīśakavarṣada 1082 neya vikramasaṁvatsarada bhādrapadamā-
sada puṇnami bṛ-

haspativāra sōmagrahaṇadaṁdu dharmmeyaṣaḍaṁgiyara magaṁ padmaṇnana magaḷu dharmmi-
yakkaṁ tāṁ māḍisideḍegē-

riya baḍagavāgila śrīlakṣmīnārāyaṇadēvaraṁgabhōgakkaṁ nivēdyakkaṁ khaṇḍasphuṭitajī-
rṇnōddhārakkaṁ ·ā dēvaralli śāstrabyākhyā-

namaṁ māḍaluṁ purāṇamaṁ hēḷalu vorbbupādhyāyargge varṣavarṣakke bāguḷiyē-
ḷu gadyāṇa poṁna jīvatakkaṁ ·ā dēvaragrā-

sanada brāhmaṇabhōjanakkaṁ śrīmadayvadiṁbargge pādapūjeyaṁ koṭṭavara kayyalu sa-
rbbabādhāparihāramaṁ sarvvanamaśyama sarvvamā-

nyamaṁ paḍedu piriyakeṟeya keḷage lālabāgina harinīrilli biṭṭaḍaketōṁṭa kaṁmaṁ nūṟe-
ppattu ·ī tōṁṭake baḍagamaṁ kariyōṇi teṁka-

maṁ kariyōṇi nīrōṇi ghaisāsarakēśavadēvana nūṟa mūvattu marakke dē[va*]ra tōṁṭada
bāviyalu nīranettiyūḍuvaru | ·ī dharmamaṁ prati-

pālisidavargge gaṁgādēviyalli sāyira brāhmaṇargge sāyira kavilegaḷaṁ koṭṭa phalavakkuṁ
ī dharmmakke kēḍaṁ māḍidavargge sāyira brāhma-

ṇarumaṁ sāyira kavileyumaṁ konda mahāpātakadōṣamakkuṁ [||*] svadattāṁ paradattāṁ
vā yō harēti vasundharāṁ ṣaṣṭirvvarṣasaha-

srāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē kṛmiḥ | dānaṁ vā pālanaṁ vēti dānācchrēyōnupālanaṁ
dānātsvarggamavāpnōti pālanādacyu-
taṁ padaṁ [||*] mattavā tōṁṭadiṁ paḍuvalu biṭṭa tōṁṭa kammaṁ 40 kaṭṭaḷikeṟeya baḍa-

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP AT THE SOUTHERN ENTRANCE OF THE ODDAPPA
(SIVA) TEMPLE AT HEMAVATI, MADAKASIRA TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1084, Vṛisha, Pushya, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa corresponding
probably to A.D. 1162 December 25 Monday. The date is not verifiable. The record
refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya-Chakravarti Vikrama (i.e., Taila III) and
mentions his feudatory Tribhuvanamalla-Mallidēva-Chōḷamahārāja as ruling over
Sīre-nāḍu from the capital Heñjeru (modern Hēmāvati). It registers the grant of
land made by the Chōḷa chief for the service of the god Dēśīśvaradēva, set up in
Heṁjeru by Periyāḷuvaseṭṭi, son of Aḻahiya-Maṇavāḷa and Āṇḍamme, of Ākūru,
a brahmadēya in Rājarāja vaḻanāḍu, a subdivision of the Chōḷa country.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 461 of 1923.)
ON A SLAB PRESERVED IN THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE GARBHA-GRIHA OF THE
NILAKANTHESVARA TEMPLE AT GOVINDAVADA, RAYADRUG TALUK, BELLARY
DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1092, Virōdhi, Chaitra, śu. puṇṇami, Sōmavāra, Sōma-grahaṇa.
The date is not regular. The given details corresponded to A.D. 1169 March 14
Friday, not Monday, as stated in the record. It refers itself to the reign of the
Chāḷukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva and mentions his feudatory Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Tribhuvanamalla-Mallidēva-Chōḷamahārāja, son of Iruṅgōḷa-Chōḷamaharaja and
grandson of Gōvindarāja. This Mallidēva, who was ruling from Gōvindavāḍi, is
stated to have made a gift of the village Santaraṇe and of lands and cesses on some
articles for the service of the god Nīlakaṇṭhēśvaradēva for whom a temple was built
by his father. Sōmēśvara wrote the inscription.

l.ka: mauḷiratnaṁ yasyāpa kaṁdukavinōdapadaṁ himādriḥ | yō dakṣiṇārṇnavamukhōdgatara-
lōkakīrttirggōviṁdarāja ·iti rājakulaikasiṁhaḥ | ·āsīdiyaṁ vasumatī vipulā ca pri-

thvī yaṁ prāpya viśvavibhavaikanidhiṁ jitāriḥ || jajñē tasmātpravitatayaśōbhūriruṁgōḷa-
bhūpaḥ

śrīṣṭhān bhūpānapi ca vidadhē nīlakaṁṭhālayaṁ yaḥ | tasyaurasō jagati viśrutaśastra-
śāstra | vijñānabhū-
vibhavaḥ khaḷu mallidēvaḥ || svasti samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāmaṁḍalēśvara | nuṟe-
ḍinalu sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttamirddu sakavarṣa 1092 neya virōdhi-
saṁvatsarada caitra suddha puṇnami sōmavārada sōmagrahaṇada mahātithiyaṁdu
sphuṭitajīrṇnōdhdhārakkaṁ dēvaraṁgabhōgakkaṁ pātrapāvuḷakkaṁ dēvara śrīpādada savopa-
dalli dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍi yaghahariyiṁ paḍuvadaḍiya saṁtaraṇeya ca-
hāṁbuḷigaya dāriyali 60 mattaru keyyumaṁ ·aḍakeyalli sojjamumaṁ ·ele hēṟiṁge
100 maṁ bhaṁḍiya battadalu baḷḷa 1 maṁ hēṟinalu mūlevattaṁ māna 1 maṁ ·aṁgaḍi-
yalu saṭṭu bhattamumaṁ | koṭṭariṁtī dattiyaṁ sarvvēśvarapaṁḍitara putraṁ kēdārē-
rorvvaru kiḍisidaḍe śrīvāraṇāsiyalli sahaśra kapileyumaṁ brāhma-
ṇarumaṁ koṁdaṁtaha mahāpātakamakku || svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō ha-
rēti vasuṁdharāṁ | ṣaṣṭirvvarṣasahaśrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ || na vi-

ṣaṁ viṣamityāhurddēvasvaṁ viṣamucyatē | viṣamēkākinaṁ haṁti(ḥ) dēvasvaṁ putrapautri-
kaṁ ||
bahubhiḥ vasudhā dattā rājabhissagarādibhiḥ | yasya yasya yadā bhūmiḥ tasya tasya tadā pha-
laṁ || svadattādviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ pūrvvadattēṣu pālanaṁ pūrvvadattāpahārēṇa svadattaṁ niḥ-
phalaṁ bhavēt || ·intī sāsanavaṁ sōmēśvaraṁ baredaṁ maṁgaḷa mahāśrī ||

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. 733 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE BAZAAR IN THE VILLAGE MADHUDI,
MADAKSIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1091, Virōdhi, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 13, Bṛihavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1169 August 7 Thursday. The month was Adhika-Śrāvaṇa. The record refers
itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king Jagadēkamalladēva ruling from Kalyāṇa.
His feudatory, the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jagadēkamalla-Mallidēva-Chōḷamahārāja, son of
Iruṅgōḷa-Chōḷamahārāja and grandson of Gōvindarasa, was ruling from Heñjeru.
The latter's civil and military officers Bammaṇa and Nāmaṇa, sons of Echimayya
and grandsons of Ayyappayya were Śaivas. Nāmaṇa who was a Tantrapāḷa built the
temple of Nāgēśvaradēva at Madhuvāḍi and endowed it with a garden and wet lands
for the service of the god and for the feeding of Brāhman tapōdhanas. The date cited
in the inscription appears to be the date on which the record was engraved and set up.
At this period the Chāḷukya dominions were under the Kaḷachuryas.

l.ka: vibhu taṁde patibrate muddibe tāyi manuca-
ḷu saṁtōṣamāgi māḍisidaṁ tāṁ vibhu nāmaṇaṁ śivā-
ya baḍagaṇa tuṁbina kāluveya baḷḷiyatōṭa khaṁ 1 kō 10
ṇa koppuradali gadde khaṁ 4 ·ūriṁ mūḍaṇa maṇalagu-
kōṭi brāhmaṇarige dānaṁgoṭṭa phalavaṁ paḍevaru || ·ī dha-
ṣavakku || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti
yāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ*] || yasya yasya yadā
bhūmi tasya tasya tadā phalaḥ | dēvasvaṁtu viṣaṁ ghō-

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. 289-A of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT CHATNAHALLI,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Vijaya, (= A.D. 1173-74). Rājaguru Daṇḍapāṇidēva
is stated to have made a gift of land to two women, Mallavve and Chandavve.

l.ka: śrīmatu vijayasaṁvaccarada pusya sudha śōmava[ra*]dalu ·uyyēsvaradēvara ttana-
ma rājaguru daṁḍapaṇidēvaru dhārāpūrvakaṁ māḍi mallave caṁdovege koṭṭaru jī-
yana keṟeya keḷaga mattar ·eraḍu baḍagiyakeṟe bhaḍagalu bhedale mattaru ·eraḍu

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1918.)
ON A PILLAR LYING NEAR THE KALLESVARASVAMI TEMPLE AT
BADA-BENNEHALLI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1095, Jaya, Prathama-Āshāḍha, Śrāvaṇa, puṇṇame, Sōma-
grahaṇa corresponding probably to A.D. 1173 June 27 Wednesday, when there was a
lunar eclipse. It refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king Jagadēkamalladēva
and records the grant of land made by Noṇambachakravarti for the service of the
god Noṇabēśvara in Harihara. It may be noted that at this period the Chāḷukya
territory was under the Kaḷachuryas.

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. 104 of 1927.)
ON TWO FACES OF A SLAB SET UP IN AIDUDEVARAGUDI AT MALAYANUR,
KALYANADRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1101, Vikāri, Pushya, śu. 13, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa.
In Vikāri, Pushya śu. 13 fell on a Thursday (not Monday). The record refers itself
to the reign of Chālukya-chakravarti (name not mentioned). The Mahāmaṇḍalē-
svara Tribhuvanamalla-Mallidēva-Chōḷamahārāja, son of Iruṅgōḷadēva and Bamma-
ladēvī, who was ruling from Heñjeru, with his queen Lakmādēvī (both very highly
eulogised) constructed five temples and set up the five gods Mallikārjunadēva,
Lakshmaṇēśvaradēva, Iruṅgōḷēśvaradēva, Mahādēvēśvaradēva and Sūryadēva at
Maleyanūr in Sīre-nāḍu and granted some lands and a part of toll revenue, collected
from the village, for the service of the gods. It is interesting to note that though the
Kaḷachuryas were the actual rulers of the Chālukya dominions now, still the
Chāḷukyas are stated to be ruling.

l.ka: yoḷu sūryyatējaṁ prati tānuṁ dhātrigellaṁ sthiradinirugaṇa(ṁ)dēvana-
durllabha | roddadagōva | khaḍgasahadēva | nigaḷaṁkamalla | vairihritsella | bhītaraṁ
kaṁṭhē yāvatkarāḷaṁ viḻasati karaḷaṁ yāvadaṁgē vibhūtiryyāvadbāhvōrbhbhujaṁ-
gā gadayugamagajā yāvadābhāti pārśvē | yāvadgaṁgā taraṁgai-
rvvahati sasikalāmēṁducūḍāsya jūṭē tāvaddōrbhyāṁ dharitrīṁ vaha-
makke lakṣmādēvi daśāphalada biṇpiniṁ ghanavādaḷu || śatapatramitravaṁ-
gi dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍi koṭṭa gadde kha 6[6*] ·a-
gi paḍuvaṇa mēre ·aḍḍagaṭṭaliṁ bara teṁkaṇa mē-
da heggāluve mūḍaṇa mēre mattaṁ dēvargge
yasya yasya yadā dattamu tasya tasya ta-
dā phalamu || na viṣaṁ viṣamityāhuḥ dē-

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. 86-A of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE NARASIMHASVAMI SHIRINE IN THE KALLESVARA
TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1103, śārvari, Pushya, śu • • • • • Ādivāra, Vyatīpāta
corresponding probably to A.D. 1180 December 21 Sunday. It records a gift of
400 kammas of land. It is incomplete.

l.ka: • • • • varṣada 1103 neya śārvvarisaṁvatsarada puṣya su • • ·ādivāra byatīpā-
• • • • • krāṁtiyaṁdu nīraguṁdada poṁmate • ḷana magaṁ śivadēvaṁ

• • • • • rāpūrvvakaṁmāḍi biṭṭaru pakṣadoḷī dharmmava • • radiṁ rakṣisi-
daṁge

l.note: The inscription stops here.

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 321 of 1905.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE INDRANATHASVAMI TEMPLE
AT CHINTALAPUTTURU NEAR PUSHPAGIRI, CUDDAPAH TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a little damaged and dated Śaka 1104, Śubhakṛit, Āshāḍha, ba.
8, Vaḍḍavāra, Karkaṭaka-saṅkramaṇa and Vyatīpāta corresponding to A.D. 1182 June
24 Thursday; f.d.t. ·45 Karkaṭa-saṅkrānti occuring on the 27th. It records
that the chief Chiddaṇadēva “lord of Kāṅchī” made a gift of some plots of land
in Kōḍūru in Muḷki-nāḍu for the service of the god Indrēśvaradēva of Puḻpagiri, after
washing the feet of Pāleyamallaya, son of Śaṅkaradēva. It also records the gift of
some wet land made by the minister, Vararāja-Nāyaka, to the same god.

l.ka: gaṁ saṁkalpavāgi tannāḷva muḷkināḍa kōḍūralli ·ūra mūḍaṇa basuri
yaṁ rājagōviṁdanu dhārāpūrvvakaṁmāḍi koṭṭaru maṁgaḷa ma-
hāśrī || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti śacīpatē saṣṭivarṣasahasrāṇi
lu sāsira kavuleymaṁ sāsira vēdapāragarumuṁ vadhisida pātakakke saluvaru ||

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 257 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE IN MANNERA-MASALAVADA,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya king Jagadēkamalladēva (who
was long dead by this time) ruling from Kalyāṇa and is dated the 4th year of the
Kaḷachurya king Rāyanārāyaṇa Āhavamalladēva, Śōbhakṛit, Jyēshṭha, paurṇami,
Sōma-grahaṇa corresponding to A.D. 1183, June 7 when there was a lunar eclipse.
Jagadēkamallā's feudatory Vijaya-Pāṇḍyadēva is stated to have been ruling over
Kōgaḷi five-hundred and Noḷambavāḍi thirty two-thousand. The Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Vikramādityarasa, together with Rāyarasa and with some Heggaḍes and Gāvuṇḍas,
is said to have made a grant confirming the one formerly made, for the service of the
god Kalidēva and placed it in charge of a certain Kalyāṇaśakti-Paṇḍita.

l.ka: svasti samastabhuvanāśrayaṁ śrīprithvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvaraṁ pa-

drārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire | kalyāṇadalnevīḍinelu sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁ-
geyyu-
ttamire || tatpādapadmōpajīvi || samadhigatapaṁca mahāśabda mahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ kāṁcīpu-
raṁ mosaḷevāḍa hanneraḍumaṁ maṁneya vikramādityarasaṁ su-
dāyattaṁ māḷke vikramāṁka ninaganavarataṁ || vinayanidhānaneṁdu suja-
na || nuḍiva beḍaṁgu nōḍuva parākrama māḍuva sāhasōkti na-
ḍava caritramuṁ hesaru rūpu guṇārnnavaneṁtu nōrppaḍaṁ kaḍugaliya-
ppa vikramana gaṁḍinadoṁdu poḍarpanēna baṇnisalaḷavalte toḍardda-
rē || biḍadaḍare poṇaruva vairiyakaḍugāyi piṁdaṭṭi muṭṭi raṇaraṁgada-
mūpaṁ || birudina ·aṁkamāle toḍarikkida kālalu gaṁḍapeṁḍaraṁ karagasagā-
ḷaniccaṇige dabbukaveṁbivanikki kādi nittarisade yōḍihōda beḷḷeraḷeya bēṁṭe-
yalu kolalu vollade kāvanu vikra[ma*]bhūbhuja || gaṁḍaragaṁḍanāha-
vada gaṁḍanumabbarisuvara gaṁḍa mārkkoḷvara gaṁḍa kūḍitappuva ma(ṁ)nneyara
havamalladēvavarśada nālkaneya śōbhakritsaṁvatsara jyēṣṭada puṇnami sōma-
rasaruṁ haggaḍeyaiceṇanuṁ heggaḍeya hollaṇanuṁ haḍavaḷa rājaṇanuṁ gauḍa-
ra gaṭṭigāvuṁḍanuṁ yi(ṁ)mmaḍigētagāvuṁḍanuṁ sēnebōva duggaṇnanuṁ samastapraje-
gaḷa muṁde śrīmūlasthānakalidēvara jīyara kētiseṭṭiyana maga sōsanayya-
na maga kalidēvaṁga kalyāṇaśaktipaṁḍitargge yūdēvargge mu(ṁ)nna naḍava bhū-
mūḍaṇa keṟeyoḷagaṇa keyi matta 1 kēśavadēvara keyiṁ mūḍaṇa ·oṁma-
reyalu yerematta 5 yā keyiṁ teṁkalu hattira ·oṁmereyalu haraḷakeyi
matta 1 ka(ṁ)mmaṁ 450 yūrayāra hūṭeyaluṁ grāmaśāntiyaluṁ dēvargge
sahasragaḍuge pūje nivēdyakke yarasaruṁ prajegaḷuṁ yā dēvara ye-
reyakeyiṁ teṁkaloṁmereyalu yere matta 1 kaṁ 450 māvinakeṟeya keḷa-
ge guttēśvaradēvara gaddeyiṁ baḍaga hattire ·o(ṁ)mmereyali(ṁ)nnēsaṟalu gadde kaṁmaṁ 100
·antere keṁgāḍu matta 9 gaddekaṁmaṁ 100 yī dharmmavanivara makkaḷa ma(ṁ)mmakkaḷu
ru śivadrōhigaḷu yī dharmmamaṁ pratipālisidavargge śrīgaṁgeyamnevāraṇāsi-

tē krimi[ḥ*] || kōṭipayaṁkavileyanekkōṭi tapōdhanara vēdavidaraṁ pannirkkōṭiya-

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 555 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE VILLAGE MALAKAPURAM,
ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a little damaged and dated Śaka 1106, Krōdhi, Kārtika, amāvāsye,
Sōmavāra, Sūrya-grahana corresponding to A.D. 1184 November 5, Monday when
there was a solar eclipse. It refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king
Trailōkyamalla Sōmēśvāradēva (IV) ruling from Jayantīpura and gives a genealogical
account of the Brāhman warriors Daṇḍanāyaka Kaḷidāsa, his younger brother
Lakshmīdēva, the former's son Bammidēva or Brahmadēva, his son Padmidēva and
the latter's maternal uncle Vatsarāja. Padmidēvarasa and Vatsarāja were ruling the
whole of the eastern country including Sindavāḍi. The village Kantarike in Bāgi
twelve, a sub-division of Sindavāḍi thousand, was granted to the temple of Brahmes-
varadēva at Kaṟekaṇṭhapura in Kīrtinārāyaṇapura, the capital of the Sindavāḍi
province. The temple was built and consecrated by the Vaḍḍavyavahāri Śrōtriya-
Māhēsvara Eṟeyamaseṭṭi.

l.ka: nātanagratanayaṁ śrīkāḷidāsaṁ pradhānalalāmaṁ tadanu prasūtanadhikaṁ śrīla-
maṁ maṁtrivaryyaṁ jagadviditaṁ tuṁbuḷa baṁmidēvanenipaṁ haṁsāṁganāvallabhaṁ [||*]
besanaṁ rāyamurāri sōmamahipaṁ pēḷalbharaṁ geyyadeydi samagrājiyo-
ḷallu mallanṛpanaṁ muṁkaṭṭidaṁ mattamormme samudrāvadhiyāge cōḷeya-
nāḍaṁ sūṟegoṁḍātanaṁ masakaṁ guṁdisi vārdhiyaṁ pugisidaṁ śrībrahmadaṁḍādhipaṁ [||*]
ldu puṭṭi vaṁśāṁbaramaṁ prabhāvisidanapratimaprabaḷapratāpi vīrāṁbudhi padmi-
dēvanene saṁdanamātya janaikabhūṣaṇaṁ || viḷasaṁnītiyutaṁ niyōganipuṇaṁ ta-
ḍanāyakaruṁ siṁdavāḍi sāsiraṁ modalāgi pūrvadiśābhāgamellamaṁ nijavī-
baraṁ salvantāgi siṁdavāḍi sāsirada kaṁpaṇaṁ bhāgi 12 ṟa baḷiya bāḍaṁ kaṁtarike-
tamāgi tribhōgābhyaṁtaraṁ sarvvanamasyamāgi dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍi koṭṭar || sā-

mānyōyaṁ dharmmasēturnṛpāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ | sarvvānē-
tānbhā-
vinaṟpārtthivēṁdrānbhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ | svadattāṁ para-
dattāṁ vā yō harēta vasundharāṁ ṣaṣṭirvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ*] |
yuvuṁ ciraniṁ baṁdaiśvaryamuṁ śauryyamuṁ mudamuṁ saṁtativṛddhiyuṁ mahime-
yuṁ nairōgyamuṁ bhāgyamuṁ paduḷaṁ śāśvatamāgi nilke vijayaṁ bhadraṁ śubhaṁ
maṁgaḷaṁ || śrīniḷayaṁ vivēki nagarāgraṇi vaiśyalalāmanuttamaṁ mānitagōtra-

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR SET UP AT THE SOUTHERN ENTRANCE INTO THE VILLAGE
OF PATASIVARAM, MADAKSIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1107, Viśvāvasu, Phālguṇa 4, Bharaṇī, Sōmavāra
corresponding to A.D. 1185 February 24, Monday on which day the 3rd tithi was
current up to ·15 and Aśvinī was current up to ·53. It refers itself to the reign of
the Chāḷukya king Tribhuvanamalla Vīra-Sōmēśvaradēva (IV) and mentions his
feudatory Bhōgadēva-Chōḷamaharāja as ruling from Heñjeru. It mentions Padma-
prabha-Maladhāridēva, disciple of Vīraṇandi-Siddhānta-Chakravartidēva, and belonging
to Dēsi-gaṇa, Pustaka-gachchha and Mūla-saṁgha.

l.note: Some lines seem to have been lost after this.
A few more lines appear to have been lost.

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 546 of 1915.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MANDAPA OF THE PARVATESVARA TEMPLE AT
NANDAVARAM, ADONI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1108, Parābhava, Pushya, śu. 13, Bṛihaspativāra, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṅkramaṇa corresponding to A.D. 1186 December 25 Thursday and refers itself to the
reign of the Chāḷukya king Vīranārayaṇa-Sōmēśvaradēva ruling from Jayantīpura.
His Daṇḍanāyaka, the brave Brahman Māḷapayya or Māḷaparasa, who was ruling over
Sindavāḍi thousand is stated to have made a gift of one gadyāṇa per month out of the
hejjuṅka collected in Guḍikallu twenty-four, for the service of the god Mallikārjuna
in Nandāvura, after washing the feet of the teacher Indraśakti-Paṇḍita.

l.ka: sōmēśvaradēvaru jayantīpurada nelevīḍinoḷu sukhadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttamire ta-
karu siṁdavāḍi sāsiramanāḷuttaviṟdu śakavarṣadinitta 1108 neya parābhavasaṁ-
kaḷci dhārāpūrvakaṁ māḍi guḍikalla 24 ṟa sthaḷada hejjuṁkadalli tiṁgaḷtiṁ-
gaḷiṁge koṭṭa gadyāṇa 1© svadattāddviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ
paradattāpahārēṇa svadattaṁ niṟphalaṁ bhavēt || yō harati kaviraśaktaścō-
ra ·ivārthaṁ parēṇa kavinā dṛṣṭaṁ | taṁ hasati sukavipadmaprabhākarō bhāskaraḥ ku-

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE AKKATANGERAGUDI AT KAMBADURU,
KALYANADRUG TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1108, Viśvāvasu, Chaitra, puṇṇame, Sōmavāra, Sōma-grahaṇa
The date is not regular. There was a lunar eclipse in Chaitra in Śaka 1108; the
cyclic year was, however, Parābhava and the week day was Saturday = A.D. 1186
April 5 Saturday. The record refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king Sōmēś-
varadēva (IV). His feudatory, the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bhōgadēva-Chōḷamahārāja, who
was ruling at Heñjeru, is stated to have made a grant of wet fields for the service
of a god (name lost). The record is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 99-A of 1904.)
ON THE FIFTEENTH SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE KALLESVARA
TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
In Śaka 1111, Kīlaka, Bhādrapada, ba. 6, Maṅgaḷavāra, Vyatīpāta corresponding
to A.D. 1188 September 13 Tuesday, the Mahājanas of Bāḷguḷi are stated to have
made a grant of one mattar of wet land for the repairs of Dāseya-Nāyaka's tank.

l.ka: svasti śrīmatu śakavarṣada 1111 neya kīlakasaṁvatsarada bhādrapada bahu-
ḷa 6 maṁgaḷavāra vyatīpāta kūḍida kapilacchaṣṭiyaṁdu śrīmatu hariścaṁdradatti-

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 348 of 1926.)
ON A ROCK NEAR THE SANGAMESVARA TEMPLE AT CHANNARAYAPATTANA,
DHARMAVARAM TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and is dated the cyclic year Jaya. It refers itself to the reign of
the Chāḷukya king Āhavamalla and mentions a gift made by him. The king cannot
be identified.
1. This inscription is engraved in continuation of No. 201.

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 316 of 1905.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING ON THE STEPS, LEADING TO THE RIVER FROM THE
EASTERN GOPURA OF THE CHANNAKESAVA TEMPLE AT PUSHPAGIRI, CUDDAPAH
TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is damaged and the date lost. It refers to the rule of the Mahā-
maṇḍaḷēsvara Trailōkyamalla-Mallidēva. His subordinate the Mahāpradhāna, Heggaḍe
Attimayya is stated to have consecrated the god Kēśavadēva in Pushpagiri and made
provision for the worship of the god.

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 346 of 1905.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE TALAKANTAMMA
TEMPLE AT DEVAGUDI, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Śukla, Jyēshṭha, amāvāsye (not verifiable). It
mentions the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bhīmasēna who seems to have been a general of
(the Chāḷukya king ?) Trailōkyamalla. The general was a devotee of the goddess
Taḍalakuṭīdēvī. A hero named Kētaya is said to have fought with Kaṭakada-
Chandradaṇḍanāyaka and died after having slain two horses and a cavalier.

l.ka: gi poḍevaṭṭu karaṁgaḷaṁ mugiyalu prasa(ṁ)nnavādaḷ nosalaka(ṁ)ṇnamūrtti
bhīmarasana taṁtrapāḷa toṁḍeyana bhāva kētayana kuṟumaṟi ·apasubā-
ḷa holamēreya sīmakke kaṭakada caṁdradaṁḍanāyakana ku-

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 470 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP AT THE ENTRANCE INTO THE COMPOUND OF THE VIRABHADRA
TEMPLE AT HOLAL, HADAGALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and not dated. It refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya
king Tribhuvanamalladēva and mentions some Daṇḍanāyakas (names lost). It
mentions some gift of land made for the service of a god (name lost). The Mahājanas
of the village Gaṇḍarādityana-Poḷal are described here as observing religious rites and
practising yōga. They were also brave warriors.

l.ka: mastaguṇasampannaru nuḍidante gaṁḍarlōkaikavīraru kalaha • • ṇḍaru piri[di*]ttu
ru pariva bhaṁḍiya kāliṁ kōvaru maṇalasaravivosevaru beṭṭiḻimita • • • •
ru javana voseya kīḷuvaru māriyatōḷa ko • • • • •

l.note: The stone is broken away after this.

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 471 of 1914.)
ON A SECOND SLAB SET UP IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is not dated and incomplete. It refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya
king Tribhuvanamalladēva and states that the hundred-and-twenty Mahājanas of
Gaṇḍarādityana-Poḷal, together with (?) Kōylūḷidēva, their servant, made a grant
of 100 kambas of land for the service of the god Kōlēśvara.

l.ka: kāliṁ kōvaru maṇalaṁ saravi posevaru beṭṭeṟimittugareva-
yoḷage nūṟu kaṁba gadeyaṁ biṭṭaru nivēdyakke

l.note: Incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF A HOUSE SOUTH OF THE KALLESVARA
TEMPLE AT SOGI, HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is very much damaged. It refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya king
Tribhuvanamalladēva and mentions the Mahāmandalēsvara Barmadēvarasa who bears
a number of birudas.

l.ka: vallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyā-

ndrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire tatpādapadmōpajīvi samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahā-
maṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ

naṁkakāṟaṁ

vanagajasūṟekāṟaṁ maleyatuḷivaṁ | gaṇḍaro[ḷjaḍi]vaṁ | rūpakandarppaṁ toṇḍamaṇḍaḷikaghaṭa-
sarppa

kamunniṟivaṁ śauryyamaṁ meṟevaṁ vāṇarāyadiśāpaṭṭaṁ subhaṭamaṇḍaḷikalalāṭapaṭṭaṁ
hoysaḷaprabaḷaśai-

l.note: The rest is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB SET UP BEFORE THE ADICHANNAKESAVA TEMPLE AT KONAKONDLA,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is not dated and is much damaged. It refers itself to the reign of
the Chāḷukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva ruling from Kalyāna. It mentions the Jaina
teachers Nayakīrtidēva and Indrakīrti, the prince Kumāra-Tailapadēva and his
subordinates Mācharasa, Saṅkarasa and Muddarasa.

l.note: The rest is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT KARAKANTHAPURA,
A HAMLET OF PEDDATUMBALAM, ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The stone on which the record is incised is broken into pieces and only two
pieces are found. The inscription has no date but refers itself to the reign of the
Chālukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva and gives the boundary of some land, evidently
a religious gift.

l.note: The stone is mutilated.
The stone is

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 728 of 1919.)
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING NEAR THE ANJANEYASVAMI TEMPLE IN
KURUVATTI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This consists of two parts and is much damaged. Its date is lost but it refers
itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva and mentions his
subordinate Mahāmaṇḍalesvara Śaṅkaradēva. From the second part it appears that
a gift of land was made.

l.note: Some more lines are broken away.

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. 497 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB LYING BEFORE THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE AT HIREHADAGALLI,
HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 771, Prajāpati, Pushya, amāvāsyā, Sōmavāra, Vyatī-
pata, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa. Śaka 771 corresponding to A.D. 849 was Śukla and
not Prajōtpatti. The Pushya Amāvāsyā of Prajōtpatti corresponded to A.D. 851
January 5, Monday; it was not the day of Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa. The inscription
refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva and states that
his feudatory, the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Māhēndra, “lord of Kāñchī,” who was ruling
the country with Marandale as the boundary, made a grant of land for the service
of the god Mūlasthānadēva in the presence of the 120 Mahājaṇas of the village
Posavaḍaṅgile. In the same cyclic year Prajāpati, Chaitra śu, 1, Sōmavāra, corre-
sponding to A.D. 851 March 7 Saturday, not Monday, a certain Malliyaṇṇa and
his father Bāchimayya are said to have made a gift of land for the service of the god
Mallikārjunadēva. In the Śaka year cited there was no Tribhuvanamalladēva
and the date is too early for any Tribhuvanamalla. Moreover the characters of the
record belong to the 11-12th century A.D.

l.ka: hēṁdraṁ sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttaṁ kōgaḷiyalirddu maraṁdale ma-
keyi tāḻiyōṇiyiṁ mūḍalu koḷabeyadāriyiṁ paḍuva nālvattaṟa pa-

ra saṁtānamallade peṟarggirasali ·ī dharmmavanagrahāraṁ sarvvabhādhaparihāravāgi rakṣi-

ssaravāgi mallikārjjunadēvargge ·ālagaṭṭada haḷuginalu biṭṭagadde ka(ṁ)mma nūṟamūvattu

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. 91 of 1904.)
ON THE 7TH SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE
IN BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and not dated. It belongs to the reign of the Pāṇḍya chief
Vijaya-Pāṇḍyadēva, whose genealogy is given in Sanskrit verse. Some Sāmanta
whose name is lost is also mentioned. Since Vijaya-Pāṇḍyadēva's elder brother
Vīra-Pāṇḍya was a subordinate of Jadēkamalla II, this record may belong to the
Chālukya period.

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE BHIMESVARA TEMPLE AT
NILAGUNDA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated the cyclic year Chitrabhānu, Pushya, śu. 10, Ādityavāra,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. It belongs to the reign of the Kalachurya king, Mahāmaṇḍa-
lēśvara, Bhujabalachakravarti Bijjaladēva. Chitrabhānu in the reign of Bijjala was
Śaka 1084 corresponding to A.D. 1162; but the week-day would be wrong for that
year; nor did the Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti fall on the day cited. The king's feudatory,
Tribhuvanamalla-Vīra-Pāṇḍyadēva who had the title “lord of Kāñchīpura” is stated
to have been ruling over Kōgaḷi five-hundred and Kaḍambaḷike one-thousand.
Under Vīra-Pāṇḍya's orders Kallimayya, the Superintendent of tolls (Suṅkad-adhish-
ṭhāyaka) in Kōgaḷi and Kaḍambaḷike, made a grant of a fixed portion of the toll-
revenue in Nīrugunda, the chief town of Kōgaḷi five-hundred, for the service of the
god Bhīmēśvaradēva in the village.

l.ka:
svasti samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ kāḷaṁjarapuravarādhīśvara suvarṇnavri-
ṣabhadhva-

ja ḍamarugatūryyanirgghōṣaṇa kaḷacuryyakuḷakamaḷamārttaṇḍa kadanapracaṇḍa mānaka-
nakācaḷa

subhaṭarādityaṁ kaligaḷaṁkuśagajasāmaṁta śāraṇāgatavajrapaṁjara pratāpalaṁkēśvara paranārī-
sahō-

daraṁ caladaṁkarāma vairībhaka(ṁ)ṇṭhīravaṁ śanivārasiddhi giridurggamalla niśśaṁka nāmādisama-
stapraśastisahitaṁ

namācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire © tatpādapadmōpajīvi || svasti samadhigatapaṁcama-
hāśabda mā-

hāmaṇḍaḷēśvara kāṁcīpuravarādhīśvaraṁ yaduvaṁśāṁbaradyumaṇi subhaṭacūḍāmaṇi nija-
kuḷakama-

vanamalla vīrapāṇḍyadēvaru noṇaṁbavāḍi mūvatticchāsirakke taleyenisida kōgaḷiya-
ynūṟu-
maṁ kaḍaṁbaḷike sāyiramumaṁ duṣṭanigrahaśiṣṭapratipālana māḍi sukhasaṁkathā-
bhujabalada cāgada neleyene negaḻdaṁ samastavasudhātaḷadoḷu || ·ene negardda kallimayya ja-

napati śrīvīrapāṁḍyadēvara besadiṁ vinutaṁ noṇaṁbavāḍiyoḷanunayadi suṁkadādhipatya-
doḷirddu
·oḍaluṁ dhanamuṁ bhāvise keḍalirddavu kiḍadakasavaradharmmamada paḍeveṁ beccarameṁde-
ḍe vuḍugade taddharmmakāryyatatparanāgi || © || svasti samastapraśastisahita śrīma-
yaynūṟu kaḍaṁbaḷike sāyirada hejjuṁka vaḍḍarāvuḷa pannāyada suṁkadadhikā-

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. 504 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE KALKANTHARAYA TEMPLE AT
KARAKANTHAPURAM, NEAR PEDDATUMBALAM,
ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Tāraṇa, Jyēshṭha, puṇṇame, Vaḍḍavāra, Sōma-
grahaṇa = A.D. 1164 June 6, Saturday, when there was a lunar eclipse. It refers
itself to the reign of the Kalachurya-Chakravarti, Bhujabalamalladēva (i.e., Bijjala).
Barmarasa, Śrīdhara-Nāyaka, Rudramayya-Nāyaka, Kahnēśvara-Nāyaka and Peggaḍe
Nāmarasa ruling over Sindavāḍi from Kīrttinārāyaṇanagarī. Eṟeyamaseṭṭi, son
of Bammiseṭṭi, set up the image of Brahmēśvaradēva. The Mahāprachaṇḍadaṇḍa-
nāyaka Bammidēvarasa is stated to have granted the village Kōsa-geyahāḻu situated
in Kandukūru twenty-four for the service of the god Brahmēśvara, in addition to
some land and an oil-mill. The Nakaras are stated to have granted a portion of
the toll-revenue.

l.ka: kahnēśvaranāyaka peggaḍe nāmarasarivarvverasu cakravarttiya besadiṁ || praṇutata-
si negaḷteyaṁ paḍeda mahāvaḍḍavyavahāri śrōtriyaṟeyamaseṭṭi pra-

• • • • ṇigaru hēṟiṁge nūṟeleyaṁ koṭṭar || v® || ·idanāvaṁ kāvanā

• • • • • • nantā tīrtthatīrāgradoḷaḷida mahāpātakaṁ tānenippaṁ || ka ||
arasara

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. 513 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT BAPURAM,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1093, Khara, Māgha, śu. 5, Sōmavāra. In this
year Māgha śu. 5 ended on Sunday (not Manday) = A.D. 1172 January 2. It
refers itself to the reign of the Kalachurya-chakravarti, Rāyamurāri Bhujabala-
malla-Sōmēśvaradēva, when the Mahāpradhāna Kariya-Kēśirājayya was ruling over
Sindavaḍi one-thousand. On this occasion the learned Mahājanas of the agrahāra
Tumbuḻa, who possessed royal honours, made a gift of land for the service of the
god Chennabīyāḷa-Sōmanātha, set up by Bīcharasayya, son of the Sēnabōva Chāvara-
sayya of the Vasisṭha-gōtra. The Mummuridaṇḍas of Tumbuḷa are also said to have
contributed cess on their commercial articles for the service of the same god.

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE MANDAPA IN FRONT OF THE
KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT KURUGODU, BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The inscription is dated Śaka 1097, Manmatha, Pushya, amāvāsye, Sōmavāra,
Sūryagrahaṇa, Uttarāyaṇa saṅkramaṇa. The date is irregular. In Manmatha there
was no solar eclipse on Pushya amāvāsye, which fell on Tuesday 13 January A.D. 1176.
It was not also the day of Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa. The record belongs to the reign of
the Kalachurya king Kuntaladēsa-chakravarti Rāyāmurāri-Sōvidēva. The Mahā-
maṇḍalēśvara Rāchamalla of the Sinda family, who was Sōvidēva's feudatory,
is said to have made a grant of land for the worship of the god Kalidēva, set up by
Kalliseṭṭi, one of the Mummuridaṇḍas in Kuṟugōḍu. The Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Ajjarasa of Baḷḷāre is said to have made a gift of land for an aravaṭṭige, and satra
for pilgirms and for the worship of the god. The Mummuridaṇḍas and the Nakharas
are said to have made a grant of taxes on merchandise. As in No. 297 the warlike
spirit of the Mummuridaṇḍas is well described.

bhavadbhiḥ | sarvvānētān bhāvinaḥ pārtthivēndrān bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāma-

ttāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēti vasundharāṁ ṣaṣṭhirvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jā-
yatē krimiḥ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP TO THE SOUTH OF THE BASAVESVARA TEMPLE AT
THE SAME VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
The inscription is dated Śaka 1099, Hēmaḷambi, Āshāḍha, ba. Kṛishṇāṅgāraka-
chaturdaśī corresponding to A.D. 1177 June 26, Sunday. It belongs to the reign of the
Kalachurya king Śaṅkhavarmadēva ruling from Kalyāṇapura. With the consent of
the Sinda chief Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara Rāchamalla, who was a feudatory of the
king, the Mummuridaṇḍas of Kuṟugōḍu constructed a trikūṭa temple and
having set up in it the image of Gavaṟēśvara made a grant of land, a
voluntary contribution of cesses levied on every article sold or purchased in the city
and of tolls, specially levied for this purpose, on every imported article. It states
that Malliseṭṭi, whose genealogy is given, set up the image of Mallikārjunadēva
and made a grant for the service of the god. It further states that Muddarasa set up
the image of Muddēśvara and made a grant of land for the service of the god.
Muddarāja's genealogy is given as follows: Mārtaṇḍadēva of Kawṇḍinya-gōtra, his sons
Sōyidēva, Rēchadēva, Kāḷidāsa and Muddarāja. The Mummuridaṇḍas are said to
have been Baṇañjigas, “the brave of the brave, protectors of the submissive, cruel
to the wicked, good to the good and conquerors of powerful enemies”.

hara śaraṇeṁbaṁ bhaktara karuṇave śaraṇeṁba śivanaḍiṁgaraneṁbaṁ hiriyaṇna sāvidēvana paramānujñe-

ttikaṁgaḷaṁ bhaktipūrvvakaṁ svadharmmadiṁ pratipāḷisi naḍasuvaru ||

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1925.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COMPOUND OF THE HANUMAN TEMPLE AT
NAGENAHALLI, RAYADURG TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The inscription is dated Śaka 1118, Naḷa, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa, corresponding
probably to A.D. 1196 December 25, Wednesday; the date is not verifiable for want
of details. It belongs to a period after the time of the Kalachurya king Saṅkama, when
the Yādavas and the Hoysaḷas were trying to take possession of portions of the
Chālukya dominions. During the regime of the Chōḷa chief Tribhuvanamalla-Bhōga-
dēva, Siṅgaya-Heggaḍe, son of Vāmarasa and Nāgaladēvi and chief of Chandeyamaṅ-
gala, situated in Kaṇiyakalla-nāḍu, is stated to have built the temples of Kannēśvara,
Bhairava and Mallikārjuna and endowed them with lands and money contributed
from taxes. Haḍapada-Rāchayya, a subordinate of Bhōgadēva, is stated to have
been administering Kaṇiyakallu-nāḍu. Mārarasa dictated and Bāchaṇṇa of Kōḷūr
wrote this inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 332 of 1912.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD AT KARSHANAPALLE, PUNGANUR ZAMINDARI,
CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is not dated. It refers itself to the reign of Sembiyan Mahābali-Bāṇa
rasa and to the rule of Vikkiyaṇṇa over Pulināḍu sixty. It records the death of
Vikki and another hero in a battle with Pallavi(a)-Dhavaḷa and that the hero's
brother Kundiga set up the stone in their memory. Sembiyan Mahābali-Bāṇarasa
was a subordinate of the Chōḷa king Parāntaka I.

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1917.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE ANJANEYASVAMI TEMPLE AT KOTTASIVARAM,
MADAKASIRA TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated in the tenth year of the reign of the Chōḷa king Rājēndra-Chōḷadēva
and records that the maṇḍapa was erected by Areyaṁ Rājarāja alias Vikramachōḻa-
Chōḻiyavariyaṁ, the chief of Chāttamaṅgalam in Tiremūr-nāḍu, and commander of the
king's army, on the 40th day opposite 160 days of the year. The inscription is
written in the Tamiḻ language and Kanarese characters.

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 329 of 1912.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD AT KARSHANAPALLE, PUNGANUR ZAMINDARI,
CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is not dated. It refers itself to the reign of the Chōḷa king Rājēndra-Chōḷa
and records the death of Naḍuḷabbe, daughter of Kittiya-Gavuṇḍa of Muttukūru.

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 534 of 1906.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE RUINED SIVA TEMPLE IN THE YENUGARATIMADI
FIELD AT PEDDA-TIPPASAMUDRAM, MADANAPALLE TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 981, Hēmaḷambi with no further detailes. Hēmaḷambi corre-
sponded to Śaka 980 current = A.D. 1057-58. It belongs to the reign of the Chōḷa king
Kō-Parakēsaripanmar Vijayarājēndra-Chōḷadēva and records that Bammarasa, son of
Kōvarmarasa of Champūru in Maḍambūr twelve, fought against a great army and
went to heaven.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. 501 of 1911.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE CHANDRASEKHARA TEMPLE AT CHIKKATI,
GUNDLUPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Ibid., Vol. III, Gl. 93.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. 502 of 1911.)
ON A PILLAR PRESERVED IN THE JUBILEE INSTITUTE AT MYSORE.
inscription.p: Ibid., Vol. IX, Nl. 1.

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT KOGALI,
HADAGALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is damaged and dated the 4th year of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-
Narasiṁhadēva, Svabhānu, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 1, Sūrya-grahaṇa. There seems to be some
mistake about the date. The details cited work out correctly for A.D. 1163 July 4,
there having been a solar eclipse on the previous day, viz., July 3 Wednesday. This
date fell in the reign of Narasiṁha I but it was not his fourth year. The next
Subhānu i.e., A.D. 1223 was the 4th year of Narasiṁha II but the details given are
not correct for that year. Some land together with some taxes are stated to have
been granted for the service of the god Sōmanāthadēva while Sōmaṇa-Daṇḍanāyaka
was the Mahāpradhāna. Some land was given also to Sōmajīyāchārya, the head
of the temple. Mention is made of the Brāhman generals Hariharadēva and
Śivakīrtidēva and their genealogy is given. Like the Mummuridaṇḍas, the 96,000
and the Seṭṭi-Guttas are described here as Udagra[vīra]-vibhavōdbhāsigaḷ, i.e., shining
with immense warlike spirit. The charity was placed in the charge of the Seṭṭi-
Guttas, the 96,000, the Mummuridaṇḍas and all the inhabitants.

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1912.)
ON TWO FRAGMENTS OF A SLAB LYING NEAR THE HONNATIKAMMA TEMPLE
AT PARIGI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1100, Viḷambi, Pausha, [pañ]chamī, Bṛihaspativāra. In
Viḷambi neither śu 5 nor ba. 5 of Pausha was a Thursday. If pañchamī is a mistake
for daśamī the date would correspond to either 19th December A.D. 1178 or 4th
January 1179. The inscription belongs to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa
(II), ruling at Dōrasamudra with his queen [Śān]taledēvī. It records the gift of a bell
to the temple of Bhīmēśvara at Guruguñjikōlabayalu near the tank of Haṟuve in
Haṟuhe-nāḍu and the grant of 800 kambas of land for the service of the god and
for the upkeep of a lamp. It is damaged.

l.note: One or two lines after this seem to have been lost.

l.ka: ṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēta vasundharā[ṁ*] ṣaṣṭirvva-

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. 90 of 1904.)
ON THE SIXTH SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE KALLESVARA
TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1116, Pramāthi, Kārtika, śu. paurṇami, Sōmavāra, Sōma-
grahaṇa corresponding to A.D. 1193, November 10, Wednesday on which day there
was a lunar eclipse; the week-day given in the record is an error. It refers itself to
the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa (II). The king is said to have made a gift
of a large tract of land when he was in the ṇelevīḍu of Bāḷguḷi, for the service of the
god Svayaṁbhu-Kalidēva of the place, after washing the feet of the five hundred
Mahājanas. The king's genealogy is given.

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1918.)
ON A BEAM IN THE MALLIKARJUNASVAMI TEMPLE AT KURUVATTI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1117, Ānanda, Chaitra, śu. 1, Śukravāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1194 March 25, Friday, in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa (II). It
records that while the king was in the nelevīḍu of Bāguḷi, he made a gift of (the whole
tract of land) called Mukkurabbe in Beṇṇeyūr twelve to Lōkābharaṇa-Munīndra for
repairs, offerings and etc. to the temple of Āhavamallēśvara. Chaṇḍarasa of Beṇṇe-
yūr twelve is mentioned.

kadanapracaṁḍa niḥsaṁkapratāpacakravartti hoysaḷa vīraballāḷadēvaru bāguḷiya nelavīḍa-

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(A.R. No. 472 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE EAST WALL OF THE VIRABHADRA TEMPLE
AT HOLAL, HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1116, Rākshasa, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 13, Ādivāra, corresponding
to A.D. 1194, July 31, Sunday; f.d.t. ·78. It belongs to the reign of the Hoysaḷa
king Vira-Ballāḷa (II) and registers the gift of land by the Mahājanas to Chauḍeya-
Nāyaka, son of Raṇaraṅgabhairava Basaveya-Nāyaka, in recognition of the service
rendered by the latter, who is said to have died in fight against a band of thieves
(chōrayūtha).

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB LYING ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE ENTRANCE TO THE CENTRAL
SHRINE OF THE BENAKAPPA TEMPLE AT ORUVAY, BELLARY TALUK, SAME
DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Vikramakāla, Ānanda, Phālguṇa, śu. 15, Ādivāra, corresponding
probably to A.D. 1195 February 26 Sunday, when a certain Ēchiseṭṭi is said to have
built a temple of Vināyaka and Immaḍi-Rāchamalladēva, son of Iruṅgōḷadēva and
grandson of Rāchamalladēva of the Sinda family, is said to have granted some land
and some amount of toll-revenue for the service of the god.

l.ka: jyaṁgeyyutirppinavā nāḍoḷ || tiḷigoḻadiṁ beḷegeyyiṁ giḷiviṁḍiṁ pūta puṟpa-

bharūpanatiguṇavaṁtaṁ dhare pogaḷe negaḷdanaṁbaliyaicaṁ || kuladīpanaicaṁge ku-
pratiṣṭheyaṁ māḍi samastadhanādidānadiṁ mānyaraṁ maṁnisi sukhadiniralu svasti

ma 1 baḍagaṇa kēriyalli nivēśana 1 koṭṭalli hoṁge mā 1 koṁḍalli mā 1 rāśige

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1918.)
ON THE WALL TO THE RIGHT OF THE SOUTHERN ENTRANCE INTO THE
MALLIKARJUNA TEMPLE AT KURUVATTI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the 7th year of the reign of Hoysaḷa Vīra-Ballāḷadēva, the cyclic
year Piṅgala, Jyēshṭha, śu. puṇṇami, Sōmavāra, Sōma-grahaṇa. In A.D. 1197 =
Piṅgala which was counted as the 7th year of Ballāḷa II, Jyēshṭha śu. 15 fell on
Sunday 1st June; it was neither Monday nor a day of lunar eclipse. The 7th year of
none of the other three Ballāḷas corresponds to Piṅgala. The king's Mahāpradhāna
Uttarākka-Heggaḍe, Mahadēva-Daṇṇāyaka of Arasiyakeṟe and the Heggaḍe Jannayya
are stated to have made a grant of certain cesses (named) raised in Kuṟuvatti, for the
service of the god Āhavamallēśvara and handed it over to Lōkābharaṇagurudēva.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] śrīmatu yādavanārāyaṇa pratāpacakravartti hoyisaṇa vīraballāḷadēva-
varṣada 7 neya

piṁgaḷasaṁvatsarada jēṣṭha suddha puṇnami sōmavāra sōmagrahaṇa byatīpāta saṁkramāṇa-
daṁdu śrīmadda-

kṣiṇavāraṇāśi kuṟuvattiya śrīyāhumallēśvaradēvara ·akhaṁḍadīvige dūpakkeṁdu śrīmanma-

gurudēvargge

yavāgi koṭṭa phalamakku ·ī dharmmamanaḷidāta śrīvāraṇāsiyalu sahasra kapileymaṁ brā-
hmaṇaruvaṁ koṁda pā-

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1918.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE FRONT MANDAPA OF THE PAMPAPATI TEMPLE
AT BENNEHALLI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The date of the record is Piṅgala = A.D. 1197-98, amāvāse, Sōmavāra, Vitīpāta.
It is not verifiable. It refers itself to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa.
Uttarāka-Heggaḍe and Janayya-Heggaḍe are said to have made a grant of
mūligadeṟe, taxes on artisans, looms and oil-mills, together with the kiṟukuḷa cess,
for offerings to the god Virūpāksha and for feeding Brāhmans. The gift was made in
the presence of the Prabhus and Gauṇḍas of Kōgaḷi-nāḍu. Uttarāka-Heggaḍe figuring
here is also mentioned in No. 323 which appears to be a record of Ballāḷa II. The
present inscription also may, therefore, belong to his reign.

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1918.)
ON THE WALL TO THE RIGHT OF THE SOUTHERN ENTRANCE INTO THE
MALLIKARJUNA TEMPLE AT THE SAME VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
This is dated the 15th year of the Hoysaṇa king Vīra-Ballāḷa, cyclic year
Krōdhana, Jyēshṭha, śu. paurṇamī, Ādivāra, Sōma-grahaṇa. In A.D. 1205 (Krōdhana),
which is sometimes cited as the 15th year of Ballāḷa II, Jyēshṭha Paurṇamī fell on
Friday (not Sunday), 9th June; nor was there a lunar eclipse on that day. Though
there was a Krōdhana in the reign of Ballāḷa III, it was not his 15th year. The
Bhaṇḍāri Sōvarasa, who was the Mahāpradhāna of Padumaladēvī, the chief queen of
the Hoysaḷa king, is stated to have made a grant of some land for the service of god
Āhavamallēśvara of Hiriya-Kuṟuvatti.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] śrīmatu yādavanārāyaṇaṁ pratāpacakravartti hoysaṇa vīraballāḷadēva-
varṣada 15 neya krōdhanasaṁ-

vatsarada jēṣṭa suddha paurṇnamiyādivāra sōmagrahaṇa vyatīpātadaṁdu paṭṭamahā-
dēvi paduma-
ladēviyara mahāpradhānaṁ bhaṁḍāri sōvarasaru śrīmaddakṣiṇavāraṇāsi hiri-
nāḍa cikkakuṟuvattivūriṁ mūḍalu beḷanakuṟuvadiṁ paḍuvalu guṁgaṟolada ka
lidēvara keyyiṁ teṁkalu guttoḷala dāriyiṁ mūḍaṇa sthaḷadalli śrīmadrājagu-
ru lōkābharaṇadēvara kālaṁ karcci dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍi koṭṭa yere guttoḷala
gaḍibadiṁ matta 6 ·ī dharmma[vaṁ ·ā] nāḍarasugaḷuṁ yalli yāvuduṁ tappade pratipā-

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. 739 of 1922.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE MANDAPA OF THE KUMARASVAMI TEMPLE ON
THE KUMARASVAMI HILL NEAR SANDUR, SANDUR STATE.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1127, Krōdhana, Phālguṇa, ba. amāvāsye, Vaḍḍavāra,
Sūrya-grahaṇa, corresponding to A.D. 1206, March 11, Saturday, when there was a
solar eclipse. It belongs to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa (II). Under
his orders, his chief minister and commander of the army, Mahadēva-Daṇḍanāyaka,
restored the grant of the village Keṟeyapaḷḷi, which Kṛishṇarāja of the Rāshṭrakūṭa
family had made formerly for daily offerings to the god Shaṇmukha, and placed it in
the charge of Vishṇukara-Brahmachāri. There is a eulogy in verse of the Yādava
race in the beginning of the record.

l.note: ga is written below the line with a kākapada.

molaviṁdī dharmma-

sya yasya yadā bhūmistasya tasya
tadā phalaṁ ||

svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō
harēta vasuṁdha-

tē krimiḥ || gāmēkāṁ ratnikā-
mēkāṁ bhūmē-

namaneṁba pātaka bhāsuratara raurava-
kke gaḷaga-

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. 55-D of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP UNDER A MARGOSA TREE AT KUDATINI, BELLARY TALUK,
SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Prabhava, Āshāḍha, ba. 15, Bṛihavāra, corre-
sponding to A.D. 1207 July 26, Thursday in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-
Ballāḷa (II). Unfortunately the record is damaged. All that can be gathered is that,
under the presidency of Vishṇukara-Brahmachāri, a learned ascetic, the five Vargas,
the Eḷkōṭi-Mahādēvas, the numberless gaṇas and the Mummuridaṇḍas assembled
together and resolved that breach of conduct and offences should be referred to the
local tapōdhana i.e., Vishṇukara-Brahmachāri.

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. 261 of 1918.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE RAMALINGASVAMI TEMPLE
AT CHIGATERI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is damaged and dated Śaka 1132 Śukla, Jyēshṭha, śu. 5, [Ādivāra] =
A.D. 1209 May 10, Sunday. It belongs to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballaḷa
(II) and states that Pommayya, the chief of Chaṇḍūru died in a battle (?)

l.ka:
svasti śrīmatu pratāpacakravartti hōsaṇa vīraballāḷadēvana rājyadalu sakavaruṣa 1132
śuklasaṁvatsarada

jēṣṭha sudda 5 ·ā śrīmatu cikkaṭe • • • • • • • • • • caṁḍu-
roḍeya • • • • • •

poṁmayya • • • • hiṁdikki māḷige • • • • • • • • • • [jitē]na labhyatē
lakṣmīḥ mritēnāpi surāṁga-
nā kṣaṇavidhvaṁsinē kaykē kā ciṁtā maraṇē raṇē || maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī ||

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE SURYANARAYANASVAMI TEMPLE AT MAGALA,
HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The inscription is dated Śaka 1131, Śukla, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, Sōmavāra, Sōma-
grahaṇa, Karkaṭaka-saṅkrmaṇa, Vyatīpāta, corresponding to A.D. 1209 July 18
Saturday when there was a lunar eclipse; the week-day cited in the record is an error.
The record refers itself to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vishṇuvardhana Ballāḷa (II),
who was ruling over Gaṅgavāḍi ninetysix-thousand, Noṇaṁbavāḍi thirtytwo-thousand
Banavase twelve-thousand, Huligeṟe three-hundred, the two Beḷuvalas, and
Māsavāḍi up to Herdoṟe. When he was camping at Haḷḷavura surnamed Vijaya-
samudra, Marmarasa, son of the Brāhman Kāḷimayya, who was one of the 200
Brāhman svāmis of Māṅgoḷa, built a trkiūṭa temple for the gods Śiva, Vishṇu and
Sūrya; and Siṅgayya, who was the Bhaṇḍāri and the 200 Mahājanas of Māṅgoḷa
made grants of land (boundaries specified) for the service of the gods. The arasu title
borne by the Brāhman Marma is noteworthy. The warlike spirit of the 200 Brāhmans
is described as that of Indra in war The genealogies of the king, of Marmarasa and
of Siṅgamayya are given. It also records other gifts made to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(A.R. No. 495 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE BHIMESVARA AND DEMESVARA
TEMPLE AT HIREHADAGALLI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1133, Prajāpati, Māgha, śu. 3, Ādityavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1212 January 8 Sunday, in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vishṇuvardhana-
Vīra-Ballāḷa (II), who was ruling over Banavāsi twelve-thousand, Noḷambavāḍi
thirtytwo-thousand, Huligere three-hundred, the two Beḷuvolas and Māsavāḍi right up
to Heddoṟe, from his neleviḍu Haḷḷavura surnamed Vijayasamudra. His senior queen
Padumaladēvī was ruling over Hosahaḍaṅgile. It records the gift of some plots of land
made at the instance of the 120 Mahājanas of the place for the two tanks, viz., Hiri-
yakeṟe and Tuṁbinakeṟe by the Brāhman general, Gaṅgimayya-Nāyaka Dāvaṇṇa-
Daṇḍanāyaka. The genealogy of the Daṇḍanāyaka is given and the Brāhman
Mahājanas of Hosahaḍaṅgile are described as ripunāyaka-santrāsigaḷu. The record
has poetical merits but is unfortunately damaged.

llāḷadēvanu noṇaṁbavāgi mūvatticchāyira banavase pannicchāyira huligeṟe mūnūṟu
beḷuvalaveraḍu māsavā-

nigrahaśiṣṭapratipāḷanaṁ mā-

ttaviral ·avara māva na

parasaradhikāravāgalavaru mukhyavāgalaśēṣamahājana nūṟippatiṁbarsvāmigaḷa divyaśrīpāda-
padmaṁgaḷige mahāpradhā-

naṁ gaṁgimayyanāyakadāvaṇnaṁgaḷu namaskāramuṁ māḍi dharmmaprasaṁgamaṁ binnapaṁ
geyyalavaru hiriyekeḻege tuṁbinakeḻegaṁ

paḍisalisuvantāgi dharmmava nīṁ māḍavēḷkeṁdu besasalmahāprasādaveṁdu kaikoṁḍūraḍeyara
śrīmānyada bhūmiya kra-

yamaṁ [koṭṭu] bhūmiyuṁ kālgarccu kaidhāreyaṁ koṁḍā mahājanaṁgaḷa saṁnidhiya-
lu dāvaṇnaṁgaḷeraḍuṁ keḻege biṭṭa dharmma

mārtimegeyi

paḍuva ·īci[me]-

yāvāḷāṇḍa | keṁgāḍa holadasīme |

ṇakōḍi || ·ūraḍagēriya baḍagavāgila maneyoṁdu || ·intinitu dharmmamaṁ mahājana nū-

viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ ||

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. 90-A of 1904.)
ON THE SIXTH SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT
BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Aṅgirasa, Mārgaśira, ba. 8, Sōmavāra, corresponding to A.D. 1212
December 17, Monday. The Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara Vīrajagadāla Bammidēvarasa, son
of Hariharadēva and grandson of Nāchidēva of Koṭṭūru, is said to have made a gift
of a large tract of land for the service of the god Kalidēvasvāmi. The gift was made
in the presence of his teacher Sōmayājya-Hiriyaṇṇa of Arasiyabīḍu.

l.note: This is written in continuation

l.ka: du mahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ koṭṭūra nācidēvana maṁma hariharadēvana putranuṁ śrīma-

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE KUMARASVAMI TEMPLE AT MUTTIGI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1136, Bhāva, Vaiśākha, śu. 1, Sōmavāra. In Bhāva, Vaiśākha
śu. 1 corresponded to A.D. 1214 April 12 Saturday (not Monday). In the previous
year, however, it began on Monday = 22nd April 1213 at ·44. The record
belongs to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballaladeva (II). It registers a gift of
land for the maintenance of a water-shed at Muttage, the chief place of Muttage
thirty, a sub-division of Kōgaḷi five-hundred, by the Brāhman Hariyaṇṇa or
Harihara, son of Vishṇu-Nāyaka, the Ūroḍeya of Ammeḷe and the Heggaḍe of
Raṅgayya, after receiving it from Boppaya-Bammaya, the Prabhu of the village.

l.ka: 1 sōmavāradaṁdu || kaṁda || vīrada balpiṁ negaḷdī dhāriṇiyo
ḍalevana • • • • mārkoḷvaṁ dhuradoḷidiri • • • • saṁharisuva kali ne-
du nāḍe celuviniṁ • • • • • • samasta • • saṁcayadinettaṁ || ka || ·aṁ-
nipa muttageya prabhugaḷa guṇastutiyeṁtene kaṁda || vibhavadoḷū-
ḷu dānaguṇadoḷu prabhu bommayyaṁge samanenippavanāvaṁ || kaṁ ||
dmōpajīvi mahāpasāyitaṁ leṁka • • • dadhiṣṭāyakanappa sāvāsi raṁgayya-
na herggaḍeyanṁmeḷeye vūraḍeya viṣu nāyakara hariyaṇnanātana guṇastu-
nnutamaṁtri hariharāṁkaṁ kṣitiyoḷu sale negaḷdaneṁdaḍē vaṇnipudō ||
·a(ṁ)ntu negaḷda hariyaṇnanu muttageya heggaḍetanaṁ māḍuvalliyalliya pra-

ṇa sūryyagrahaṇadalu biṭṭa • • • • ḷḷiyadāriyiṁ teṁkalu mū-
ḍaṇa mēreyereyabāgu teṁkaṇamēre • • • paḷḷapaḍuvaṇa mēre doḍḍabadu-
hu baḍagaṇamēre yīśāṁnya • • • • • • yiṁtu caturasra kōga-

ṭṭa phala yī dharmmamanapaharisidavaru gaṁgeya taḍiyalu gōvu brāhmara-

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE VENUGOPALASVAMI TEMPLE AT MAGALA,
HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1136, Bhāva, Chaitra, śu. 11, Bṛihaspativāra. The
late Mr. Swamikannu Pillai has remarked on this date as follows:—“= A.D. 1215
Thursday March 2; ·48. N.B. A.D. 1214-15 was Bhāva and Śaka 1136 but the
Chaitra here referred to is Chaitra at the end not Chaitra at the beginning of the year
(vide section III of Indian Chronology)”. But it may be noted that it was not a
day of Saṅkramaṇa. The inscription refers itself to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king
Vishṇuvardhana Vīra-Ballāḷa (II). The Uroḍeyas Harihara and Gōvinda, two learned
Brāhmans, are stated to have consecrated the image of Śrī-Gōpāla and, with the
Mahājanas of Māṇgoḷa, made grants of land and houses for conducting the worship of
the god. The genealogy of Harihara and Gōvinda is given and the Brāhman
general and minister of the king named Mādhava is praised as a great warrior.

l.ka:

gatō yasya mēruḥ

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. 68-A of 1904.)
ON A SLAB LYING ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE ENTRANCE OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
OF THE BENAKAPPA TEMPLE AT ORUVAY, BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Īśvara, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 30, Śukravāra, Sūrya-grahaṇa,
corresponding to A.D. 1217, August 4 Friday, with a solar eclipse. It belongs to the
reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa (II). Raṇadhavala Basavaṇa-Daṇḍanāyaka,
who is described as the rescuer of Ballāḷa's suzerainty, is stated to have granted some
land for the service of the god Vināyaka. Ēchaseṭṭi is stated to have granted the
Vināyaka-sthāna to Chīlarāśi-guru, the disciple of Bhīmarāśi-guru; the sthala of
Kaṁpile and the four Nāḍus made a gift of tolls on bullocks to Echaseṭṭi.

l.ka: vaḷa basavacamūpaṁ dhārāpūrbbakaṁ māḍi koṭṭa keyi teṁ-
śige koṭṭanu kuṟugōḍa kaṁpileya sthaḷavuṁ nālkunāḍuṁ ·etti-
naśāsanavaṁ ·ēcaseṭṭige mānyaṁ māḍi koṭṭaru || svadattāṁ pa-
radattāṁ vā yō harēti vasundharāṁ || ṣaṣṭirvvarṣasaha-

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. 743 of 1922.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE MANDAPA OF THE RUINED SIVA TEMPLE ON THE BANK OF
THE NARIHALLA AT TALURU, HOSPET TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Bahudhānya, Jyēshṭha, ba. 15, Ādivāra corresponding to A.D. 1218
June 4, Sunday, in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa (II). The Mahānāḷprabhu
Bomma-Gavuḍa of Yaguvaṭṭi made a gift of land to Bommōja for constructing the
Iśvara temple of Talara.

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE BOLU-BASAVA TEMPLE AT KUDATINI,
BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1140, Bahudhānya, Kārtika, puṇṇami, Bṛihaspativāra, Sōma-
grahaṇa. In Bahudhānya = Śaka 1140 (expired) Kārttika śu. 15 corresponded to
A.D. 1218 November 4, Sunday (not Thursday); but in the next year i.e., Pramāthi,
Kārtika Puṇṇame did fall on Thursday = 24 October A.D. 1219. Even on that day
there was no eclipse. The record belongs to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-
Ballāḷa (II). When Udayākaradēva-Brahmachāriyarasa, the five vargas of the sthāna
of Koṭṭitoṇe, the ascetics of the five maṭhas and numberless gaṇas had assembled, the
sixty families of the oil-makers set up the god Telligēśvara and made a gift of the
cess levied on every sale and purchase, for the service of the god. The gift was
made after washing the feet of Kalyāṇaśakti, a Śaiva teacher in charge of the
institution.

l.ka: gu hoṁge kaṭṭidalagina cikkatārava teṟuvaru beḷiya tāradarddhada ||

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1918.)
ON A BEAM IN THE MALLIKARJUNASVAMI TEMPLE AT KURUVATTI,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This undated record refers itself to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa.
The Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gommadēva, expert in śāstra and śastra, and promoter of the
glory of the Yādavas, is said to have made a gift of 36 mattars of land to the north of
Haṟuhe in Beṇṇeyūru twelve for the service of the god Āhavamallēśvara and placed
it in the charge of Lōkābharaṇa. Since this Lōkābharaṇa is also mentioned in a
record of Ballāḷa II, the present inscription may belong to that king's reign.

l.ka: ·ōṁ namaḥ śivāya || jayatyāhavamallēśaḥ | śēkharīkṛtacaṁdramāḥ || yatpādapaṁkajē nitya-

yādavakuḷakumudinīniśākaraṁ | satyaratnākaraṁ | nīticāṇikyaṁ | ·amōghavākyaṁ | śaṁkha-
cakragadādharaṁ sakaḷa-

kaḷādharaṁ | siṁdasiguṇānvayaprasūtaṁ | lōkavikhyātaṁ | caladaṁkarāmaṁ | bhujabaḷabhīmaṁ
asahāyasamarttha

kke be(ṁ)ṇneyūrppa(ṁ)nneraḍaṟa baḷiya bāḍaṁ | haṟuheyiṁ baḍagalu pāṁḍyadēvana balma-
nuśya-

ru be(ṁ)ṇneyūra caṁḍugidēvanuṁ kāḷagauḍanuṁ || haṟuhegiduttaravenisalkuṟuvattiya
dakṣiṇaṁ svapūrvva-

mūṁvattaṟu mattaru keṁmogeyaṁ neṟe lōkābharaṇacaraṇabhaktiyoḷittaru || [maṁgaḷa]
śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(A.R. No. 320 of 1925.)
ON A STONE PLANTED IN FRONT OF THE MURUKAL-MATHA AT KOTTUR,
KUDLIGI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Vishu, Āshāḍha, ba. 5, Sunday, corresponding to
A.D. 1221, July 11, Sunday. Hence the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Narasiṁha to whose
reign the record belongs may be the second of that name. His general, Kēśava-
Daṇḍanāyaka, who is stated here to have been the cause of the expansion of his king-
dom, made a gift of the village Māvinamāge to Śaṅkaranārāyaṇa-Bhaṭṭa, Bhēruṇḍana-
Bammayya, Achyuta-Bhaṭṭa and to Nāga, (son) of Chīlaya-Nāyaka for the purpose of
maintaining a satra (feeding house) and appointed the Mummuridaṇḍas, Hariga Kāḷa-
Bōva, Jagaddaḷa-Bammadēva's son Sōyidēva and Ghaṭṭidēva for the protection of the
charity. Sēnābōva Sōmayya wrote this and the mason, Aṇṇōja engraved it. We
know from other records that Kēśava was a Brāhman general. It also records a grant
of land to the god Uḍakēśvaradēva by the Mantri Muktidēva.

l.ka: ṇaneṁbaṁkada bhaṭṭaṁge saṁda māvinamāgeyaṁ || sōmaṁ suvikramapāḷaraṇō-
vade koṁdikki beṁbeyaṁ kēśavanu bhūvinutarosedu koṭṭaru māvinamā-
doḷī koṭṭūra haḷḷi māvinamāgeyaṁ mudadiṁ jagadoḷ sōyi-
jarāja malaparoḷu gaṁḍa kadanapracaṁḍa yādavakuḷadvimaṇi sadrājacūḍā-
bhīma parabaḷasādhaka beṁbidēva kēśavadaṁḍādhipaṁ kādi geludaṁ viṣusaṁvatsa-
neṁba bhaṭṭaṁge bhēruṁḍana baṁmayaneṁba bhaṭṭaṁge yacyutaneṁba bhaṭṭaṁge cīlaya-
nāyakana nāgaṁge māvinamāgeyeṁba haḷḷiyaṁ koṭṭa satrakke yera-
ḍu haṁneraḍaṟa prabhugaḷu muṁmuridaṁḍa paṭṭaṇasvāmigaḷu haṭṭa-
gāṟa hariga kāḷabōva maṁḍaḷika jagaddaḷa baṁmidēvana suputra
sōyidēva ghaṭṭidēva yī dharmmavaṁ svadharmadiṁ pratipāḷisidaru yī dharmmakke yā-
ṇaruvaṁ sāyira kavuleya koṁdaṁtaha naraka ·ī dharmmavaṁ yārayi-
du rakṣi[si*]davaṁge yā brāhmaṇiriṁge yā gaṁgātīradalliyā kavulegaḷaṁ ko-
ṭṭa phala yī sāsanavaṁ yayyāvaḷaya sēnabōva sōmayya yī sā[sa*]na-
paṁ khaṁḍaḷisidāta kalukuṭiga ·aṇnōja maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī ©
śrīmatuḍakēśvaradēvarige maṁtri mūrttidēvaru tāvu haḍada keyiṁ holagēri-
ya paḍovalu mattaru 4 beṁbēśvarada gadeyiṁda dēvara baḍagaṇa gade 270 kāni • • •
• dhārāpūrvvakavāgi koṭṭaru ·ā dēvara tapōdhana baicajīya ·ātana taṁma malla-
jīya taṁma sakalasāmyadoḷage tamage ·eraḍu bhāge mantramūrttigaḷigevera-

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(A.R. No. 281 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD TO THE SOUTH OF ANIJIGERE,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1145, Chitrabhānu, Pushya, ba. 8, Sōmavāra, Uttarāyaṇa
saṅkramaṇa, Vyatīpāta corresponding to A.D. 1222, December 28, Monday, on which day
the tithi commenced after ·52; the Śaka year was current. The inscription refers itself
to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Nārasiṁhadēva. When his Mahāpradhāna
Ammaṇṇa-Daṇṇāyaka was administering all the dēśas, the latter's subordinate, Asa-
gōḍa Pallava-Sāvanta of the Sonda-kula, who was the Māhānāḷprabhu of the Sāvira-
nāḍu made a gift of some plots of land (specified) for the service of the god Kalidēva
in the village Kūchigōḷa, which was situated in Uchchaṅgi thirty. Both Ammaṇṇa
and Asagōḍa Pallava are said to have been great warriors.

l.ka: datti sakavarṣada [1]145 neya citrabhānusaṁvatsarada puśya bahu-
ṇa mattareraḍu ma 2 maṁ śivatīrtthada nīrubhūmi kīḷubhū[mi]
japaṁḍitara kālaṁ karcci dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍikoṭṭanintī dharmmama-
ta dānamāḍida phalamakku ·iṁtī dharmmamanāru keḍisidaḍaṁ vāra-

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1913.)
ON THE FOURTH SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD IN THE BHIMESVARA
TEMPLE AT NILAGUNDA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1145 Subhānu, [Mā]gha, śu. 11, Bṛihaspativāra corresponding
to A.D. 1224 February 1 Thursday, f.d.t. ·87. It belongs to the reign of the Hoysaḷa
king Narasiṁhadēva (II). His father Ballāḷa is stated to have driven out single-
handed the Sēvaṇa army from Soraṭūr up to the bank of the Kṛishṇavēṇī river,
being mounted on his elephant. It registers the gift of some village (name lost) for
the service of the god Bhīmēśvara.

l.ka: maṁ nūṁki beṁnaṭṭi koṁdaṁ soraṭūriṁ kriṣṇavēṇiya nadiya ta-
magaranaṁ pāṁḍyanaṁ koṁdanu nāmadama • • • paṭṭamanadaṁ cōḷaṁge muṁ-
śakavaruśa 1145 neya svabhānusaṁva • • • • gha su 11 bruhavāra-
rvvakaṁ māḍikoṭṭu ·ā dharmmavanu mūnūrbbaru dēvara śrīkāryyava na-
jayaśrīyumakke yidaṁ kāyade kāyiva pāpige kurukṣētraṁgaḷoḷ
ḷū || svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharā[ṁ*] śaṣṭirvaruṣasaha-
srāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi[ḥ*] || maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrīśrīśrī

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1914.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE MUKHAMANDAPA IN THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT
SOGI, HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the 6th year of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Narasiṁha, the cyclic year
Vyaya, Chaitra, śu. 10, Sōmavāra. The date works out correctly only for A.D. 1226,
March 9 Monday, f.d.t. ·44. And so the king in whose reign the record is dated must be
Vīra-Narasiṁha II. Under the orders of Bommaya-Daṇṇāyaka, the Mahāpradhāna
of the king, and with the consent of Gōparasa, Bhūtarasa, Hēmayya and Maṅgaṭeyya,
Siripaṇṇa, the officer of tolls, Mailāradēva and others made a grant of 2 gadyāṇas
each year out of the tolls-revenue in Pāṇḍya-nāḍu for the service of the god Kalidēva
of Sōge. Similarly the Heggaḍe Dēvayya and others made a grant of 2 gadyāṇas for
the service of the same god under the orders of Hiriyaṇṇa, the Heggaḍe of Boppaya-
Daṇṇāyaka, who was the officer of the bilukoḍe tax.

l.ka: da(ṁ)ṇnāyakara besadiṁ gōparasa bhūtarasa tigaṭeyyana hēmayya
ttiyā pāṁḍyanāḍa paḍikēṇiya sthaḷada kōgaḷināḍa davasā-
yadoḷage variśaṁprati nibaṁdhavāgi saluva gadyāṇaveraḍuyā bi-
ḷappaṇeyiṁ heggaḍe dēvaya koṁmaya nellakūḷa mādayya-
ṁ[ga*]ḷuṁ yā davasāyadalu koṭṭa gadyāṇameraḍu ·anta ga 4-
dharmmavanu pāḷisuvaru pāḷisadirdaḍe vāraṇāsilu kavileya koṁda-

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(A.R. No. 449 of 1914.)
ON ANOTHER PILLAR IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This record is of the same date and belongs to the same king as No. 447 of 1914.
It mentions the same personages. The god for whose service gadyāṇa 1 and paṇas 10
were granted is Kōḍiya-Sōmanāthadēva at Sōge.

l.ka: di bommayada(ṁ)ṇnāyakara besadiṁ gōparasa būtarasa tigaṭeyya-
suṁkādhikāri siripaṇnamaylāradēva divakaradēva mallaya heggaḍegaḷu sō-
geya kōḍiya śrīsōmanāthadēvara raṁgabhōganivēdyakaṁ biṭṭa dattiyā
pāṁḍyanāḍa paḍikēṇiya sthaḷada kōgaḷināḍa davasāyadoḷage vari-
śaṁprati nibaṁdhavāgi saluva gadyāṇa 1 pa 5 yā bilukoḍeyadhi-
ggaḍe dēvayya koṁmayya nellakūḷa mādayyagaḷu yā dava-
sāyadalu koṭṭa pa 5 ·āvadhikāri baṁdaḍaṁ kēṇivoḷageḷihi pra-
tipāḷisuvaru yo dharmmavanu pāḷisuvaru pāḷisididdaḍe vāraṇā-
si·ālu kavileya koṁdaru maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrīśrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1925.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT BENNIKAL, KUDLIGI
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1148, Byaya, Chaitra, śu. 11, Ādityavāra, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkra-
maṇa, Vyatīpāta. The date is irregular. Chaitra śu. 11 in Vyaya corresponded to
A.D. 1226 March 11, Wednesday (not Sunday); nor was it a day of Saṅkramaṇa.
It belongs to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Narasiṁhadēva (II). It extols the
Hoysaḷa dynasty and traces the king's descent from Saḷa and states that the Mahā-
pradhāna A2mitayya-Daṇḍanāyaka, son of Vāmarasa of the Bhāradvaja-gōtra and
Kāṇv-ānvaya, and a great general and warrior, constructed the temples of Padmalēś-
vara, Amṛitēśvara, Lakshimīnārāyaṇa, Vāmēśvara, Ballāḷēśvara and Narasiṁha at
Beṇṇekallu, the chief town of Beṇṇekallu twelve in Kōgaḷi five-hundred in Noḷamba-
vāḍi-nāḍu and that he made grants of land in several villages for their worship, main-
tenance and repairs of the temples. The grants are said to have been made by the
king in the presence of the gods Vajrēśvara and Sōmanātha, after washing the feet of
the Śaiva teacher Khaḷēśvaradēva, the Rājaguru. The genealogy and exploits of the
Brāhmaṇ Daṇḍanāyaka Amitayya, as well as the spiritual descent of Khaḷēśvara are
given in elegant Kannaḍa poetry.

nu nṛsiṁhaṁ magarādhirāyalayakāḷaṁ cōḷanistārakaṁ || kaḍitakkēṟittu nālkuṁ kaḍala
kaḍevaraṁ dhātri kīrttisrajaṁ sōrmmuḍiga-

rvaḍedarvāmarasaṁ lakumarasavamaradaṁḍādhīśaṁ || ·ā

paḍeda putranamitacamūpaṁ || moneyoḷu matkarakhaḷgaṁ

pēsaṇahanumaṁ || vritta || ·iṟiyalu kaluta

baḷikkavilla saribhaṁgaṁ śauryyadoḷu cāgamaṁ meṟeyalu katta baḷikkavilla kalekaṁdavudā-
ryyadoḷu permmeyiṁ peṟarārppoysaḷarājyadoḷu pesarggo-

rāyanṛsiṁhapēsaṇahanūmaṁ vīradoḷu cāgadoḷ

himasētupratibaddhamappa jasamaṁ kaykoṁḍanā taṁdeyiṁde magaṁ vīranudāraneṁba guṇadiṁ-
dōpirddapaṁ taṁdegoṁdu mukhakaggaḷamāda ṣa-

kadanaṁ bokkabaḷikkavā raghujanaṁtā pārththanā bhīmanā mada-

yanā ṣaṇmukhaṁ || suraguru lagnamaṁ kuḍe viriṁci cirā-

maṁtriballugige puṭṭidanī narasiṁhanaḷkaṟiṁ sthiratarapu-

ṇyamūrtti kuladuṁnatapuṁṇyada puṁjameṁbinaṁ || mattaṁ vamitacamūpana nāmāvaḷi yenteṁ
daḍe || mahāpracaṁḍadaṁḍanātha lakṣmīdharadēva vīralakṣmīvi-

ḷāsāvāsa pradhānakarikaḷabhayūthanātha śrīmadamarēśvaradēvara kīrttinarttakīnarttanaviśā-
ḷaraṁga sakaḷakuḷarājitavājivaṁśacūḍāmaṇi vāmarājapriyatanū-

jāta rūpu rēkhe thāpane baysike toḍemaḍakaydaniyeṁba caḍāyatikegāyatikevaḍedārū-
ḍhavidyāviḷāsa | sabaḷada sellahada kuṁtadahathaḷada kudure-
