seṇasi maccaripaṁge ta(ṁ)nnavāruvamaṁ vi(ṁ)nnavittu mu(ṁ)nna pārvi-

si murāriyoḷu dekhkhādekhkhiyoḷu kādi taḷtiṟidu geldu turugadaḷamaṁ taṁda pagemeccegaṁ-
ḍa dīnānāthakathakapu(ṁ)ṇyapāṭhakādinānāyācakajanajaṁ-

gamapārijāta bhaṭṭajanadāṁdhava boppanaṁkakāṟa | kaṁdukanīṟaraṇaraṁgasiṁga veḍeyaveḍaṁga
murārithaṭṭadiśāpaṭṭa saṁgrāmajattalaṭṭa caladaṁkarāma vīrana-

rasiṁhapēśaḷahanūmaṁta vīraviḷāsinīnayanakāṁta vāranārīcittacamatukārarūpa śrīmada-
mitacamūpa sthiraṁ jībhyāt || khurapītābhrāṁgaṇaṁ

siṁgaṇanripana hayaśrēṇi kaḷpāṁtadhārādharamallāmalliyoḷu yādavagajaghaṭeyoḷu taḷtu-
dāyuddhamadhyakkuriliṁgaṁ nilvavolu niṁdēḷaṟe pēḷaṟe

mārkkoṁḍu beṁkoṁḍu koṁḍaṁ turagabrātaṁgaḷaṁ nēṟiligeyoḷamitaṁ daṁḍanāthāṁjanē-
yaṁ || ·iṟida raṇaṁgaḷiṁ taṇida rōcakamāytu

javaṁge nāradaṁ neṟe kalahēkṣaṇakkalasidaṁ bisunettara nakkisalu nelaṁ neṟeyadu bhūtakōṭi
peṇanaṁ neṇanaṁ baritīvi tiṁdu kaṭṭaṟaveyanāntuveṁtamitanaṁ po-

gaḷveṁ pagemeccegaṁḍanaṁ || manu kaliyalla śauci daśakaṁṭanaṇaṁkali śauciyallagurvvina sura-
siṁdhusonu kaliyuṁ śuciyuṁ patibhaktanalla neṭṭane diṭadinda saṁda

kaliyuṁ śuciyuṁ patibhaktiyuktanuṁ hanumanadōrvvanaṁdamitanindu vicārisuvaṁ-
du dhātriyoḷu || ·asadriśaviṁdumaṁḍaḷavadiṁdamri-

tōjvaḷakīrttikāṁtigīkṣise kaṟevuḷḷudoṁdu kaṟeyilladudoṁdaṟiṁdavaṁdu hōlisidape-
niṁdu nāḷe ripuharmyataḷaṁgaḷa mēle taḷtapervvasalege pāyguviṁ-

duvamritaṁ baḷikaṁdeṇe caṁdramaṁḍaḷaṁ || viditakaḷaṁkanallada kaḷādharanuddhatagōtravairiyallada
vibudhādhipaṁ kuvaḷeyadviṣanalladinaṁ dvijihvanallada

nutabōgi bhaṁgayutanallada cāruguṇāṁburāsiyeṁbudu jagavarththiyiṁdamitanaṁ
dhuradoḷu pagemeccegaṁḍanaṁ || sanivāradoḷīyalu kalutinavāra-

doḷu jayippa daṁḍādhiparaṁ nenavude pēśaḷahanumanananavaratavinōdadāniyaṁ hogaḷveḍe-
yoḷū || pratidinavudyāpanamukhyateyiṁ halavu brataṁ

gaḷaṁ cariyisiyuṁ vitaraṇavoṁdane janmabrataveṁdenutippudāranamitacamūpaṁ || dhareyoḷaja-

hōtkaramanene māḍisida vāmarasana sutanamitadaṇḍanāyaka hanumaṁ || vritta || © gaṁbhīra-
sphāravēḷābahaḷaparivriḍhallōlakallōlamālāraṁbhaprōḍḍīnaphēna-

·aṁtā samudramudritamaṁteḷeyoḷagoppi tōrppa jaṁbūdvīpakkaṁtalli madhyamēruviniṁ
teṁkalu bhōgabhūmi bhāratavarṣaṁ || ·aṁtā bharatāvaniyoḷu kuṁta-

ḷadēśaṁ nisarggakāmadughaṁ tāneṁtene noṇaṁbavāḍiyaneṁtabjaja kīrttisalu samartthanē
jagadoḷu || sāgaradaṁtajasrajaḷapūrṇataṭākacayaṁgaḷiṁ vanaṁ pūga-

mahīruhaṁ kadaḷiteṁgulavaṁgatamāḷajāḷadiṁde bāgi phalaṁgaḷiṁdeseva kayvolanoppuva gaṁdha-
śāḷiyiṁdāgaḷu be(ṁ)ṇnekallesedu tōrppudu kōgaḷināḍo-

ḷartthiyiṁ || mattaṁ || sirisaṁ kaṁmmaranīḷe bāḷe vakuḷaṁ dāḷiṁbavaśchādatāḷage nēṟilphanasāṁ
bravatti tiḷakaṁ kaṁkellapu(ṁ)nnāga pādari pūgadṛma kauṁgu teṁ-

gu badari kharjjūra nāraṁga kēsarameṁdiṁtivaṟiṁda raṁjisivududyānaṁ purōpāṁtadoḷu ||
kaṁda || śukapikamarāḷakēkiprakarecakōradvirēphasaṁkuḷada ravaṁ

prakaṭisaludyānavanaṁ makaradhvajanikkedāṇamene sogayisukuṁ || © svasti samastabhuvanā-
śraya śrīprithvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēsvara dvārāva-

tīpuravarādhīśvaraṁ yādavakuḷāṁbaradyumaṇi samyaktacūḍāmaṇi malerājarājanaprati-
matēja malaparoḷgaṁḍa gaṁḍabhēruṁḍanasahāyaśūra maṁdaradhīra śa-

nivārasiddhi śaraṇāgatavārddhi giridurggamalla vairiśirasella caladaṁkarāmanahitagajabhīma
cōḷarāyarājyōddhāraka magarādhirāyasainyasaṁhāraka cōḷana-

ni dōrasamudrada nelevīḍinoḷsukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ prithvīrājyaṁgeyyuttamire ©
tatpādapadmōpajīvi śrīmanmahāpradhānaṁ mahāpasāytaṁ yādavarā-

yadakṣidakṣaṇabhujadaṁḍaṁ pagemeccegaṁḍaṁ rāyanarasiṁhapēśaṇahanūmaṁtaṁ hayārūḍha-
rēkhārēvaṁtaṁ sabaḷatrinētra sajjanamitranārūḍhavidyāviḷāsa vīravi-

jayalakṣmīnityanivāsanamitayyadaṁḍanāyakaru noṇaṁbavāḍināḍoṇagaṇa kōgaḷiyayi-
nūṟaṟa beṁṇekallu haṁneraḍakkaṁ mukhyavāda be(ṁ)ṇneka-

llagrāmadalu tāvetisida dēvatālayaṁgaḷalu suppratiṣṭitarāda śrīpadmalēśvaradēvaraśrīmada-
mitrēśvaradēvara śrīlakṣmīnāraṇadēvara śrīvāmēśvaradēvara śrīballā-

ḷēśvaradēvara śrīnarasiṁhadēvara ·aṁgabhōgaraṁgapūjegaṁ nityanaimittikanaivēdyakaṁ caitra-
pavitrādipaṁcaparvvaṁgaḷgaṁ khaṁḍasphuṭitajīrṇnōddhārakaveṁdeṁdiṁgaṁ salvaṁtāgi pāda-
pūje-

ya tettu śrīvīranārasiṁhadēvara śrīhastadiṁ śrīvajrēśvaradēvara sōmanāthadēvara sa(ṁ)nni-
dhānadoḷu śakavarṣa 1148 neya byayasaṁvatsara caitra suddha ·ēkādaśī ·ādi-

tyavāravuttarāyaṇasaṁkramāṇa byatīpātadaṁdu beṁṇekallabhūmiyoḷage moraḍiya mā-
ciseṭṭiya ciṁcagaṭṭada keḷagaṇa guyyala gadde mattaru 2 kaṁba 644 kuṁ-

bāṟara guyyalagadde kaṁba 539 maṇiyamagauḍana mācagauḍana keṟeya keḷagaṇa gadde matta-
ru 3 gadde kaṁba 62 ·aḍakedōṭa kaṁba 700 ·antu mattaru 7 kaṁba 845 ·ā beddalūra
muṁdaṇakeyi

mattaru 2 holagēriya baḍagaṇa keyi mattaru 4 kaṁba 706 kuṁbāṟara guyyala ciṁcaga-
ṭṭada baḍagahāḷa keyi sahita mattaru 6 kaṁba 97 ·antu keyi mattaru 12 kaṁba 803 ·ā
bhūmiya

catusīme mūḍalu hebbuṇuseya haḷḷa vuppina mōḷa teṁkalu kaṟuṁbagaddeya bāyikalu
ciṁcagaṭṭanāyakara guyyala haḷugina haḷḷada mūḍaṇa baduhina naṭṭa kalu

mēre paḍuvalu māḍūriṁge hōha heddāri mēre baḍagalu ṟoṭṭiyahaḷḷa mēre || ·i dēva-
rgge beṁṇekallu haṁnaraḍaṟoḷagaṇa haḷḷi māniliymaṁ sarvvābādheparihāra sa-

rvvanamaśyamāgi·ācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluvaṁtāgi dhārāpūrvvakavāgi haḍadu biṭṭa datti ||
ā māniliya catusīme || mūḍalu ṟoṭṭiyahaḷḷa kūṁṭeyakoḷa | teṁkalu do-

ḍḍēriya koḷa | malliyabbeya koḷa paḍuvalu mallana moraḍi | baḍagalu hagare mēre ||
svasti śrīmadakhiḷabhuvanajanajanitasaṁsārapārāvāramadajaḷamahāprayā-

ḍaruṁ | tapaprabhāvatējōmārttaṇḍaruṁ | ·a(ṁ)nnadānavinōdaruṁ | nikhiḷadharmāhlādaruṁ |
abhinavapāśupatasamayavārddhivarddhanasudhākararuṁ | mērusannibhasthiraruṁ | svakīyā-
nu-

leyāḷadēvabratikuḷakamaḷārkastatpraśiṣyarssapūjyāṁnvita bhuvi jayatu śrījñānarāsi-
rmmunīśaḥ tatsiṣyastāpasaḥ srēṣṭhaḥ sōmēśvaranahābratiḥ tanmuniśiśyavikhyātō

śāṁtarāsiryyatīśvaraḥ || kaṁda || ·ā muniya śiṣya bhṛgviśvāmitrāṁgirasakapilasanakādimuni-
stōmarttamageṇeyenalaṁtā mahimeyaṁ tāḷdidaṁ kaḷēśamunīśaṁ || ·ava-

ra śiśya || jayatu budhanidhānaḥ | kalpadhātrījadānaḥ | bratikuḷasuradhēnuḥ | sūrivaktrābja-
bhānuḥ || prakraṭavimaḷakīrttiḥ | pu(ṁ)ṇyapuṁjaikamūrttiḥ | svamatajaḷadhicaṁdraḥ | śrī-

kaḷēśabratīṁdraḥ || vritta || dhāriṇiyoḷu virājisuva kaḷpakujaṁ jeḍevottudō | rasādhārama
tāḷditō parusalākuḷamaṁ dharisittō mēkhaḷāsārama kāmadhēnu ta-

ḷayittene tāpasavēsadiṁda lakṣmīramanī kaḷēśvarana kīrttisuvarbbudharoldusaṁtataṁ || sma-
rana samā • • • ḍaṟi tāpasanādanō kaḷpabhūruhaṁ dhareyoḷe kalkigaṁdugidu tāṁ ya-

tiyādanōgastyanudghadeccaradoḷe mēre baḷki vratiyādanō pēḷenalopputirppanāśca-
ryya munīśvaraṁ nikhiḷalakṣmigadhīśvaranī kaḷēśvaraṁ || mattamī dēvarggaṁ dēvara datti-
sthaḷakaṁ

kōgaḷiyainūṟaṟa tapacakravartti rājaguru kaḷēśvaradēvara śrīpādaprakṣāḷanaṁmāḍi mahā-
pradhānaṁ ·amitaiyyadaṇnāyakaru śrīmatpratāpacakravartti śrīvīranārasiṁhadēvara
kayya-

lu dhārāpūrvvakaṁ māḍisikoṭṭaru || mattavā ba(ṁ)ṇnekallu hanneraḍaṟa ·amiteyanāyaka
haricaiyanāyakanu vūroḍeyaruṁ ·ā ha(ṁ)nneraḍu haḷḷiya

gauḍugaḷuṁ ·eṁṭu hiṭu byasavakaḷu mukhyavāgi ·ā ·amiteyanāyakana kayyalu dhārāpū-
rvvakaṁ māḍisikoṭṭaru || ·i dēvarige saṁteyāya koḍakkarevānaṁ-

ṇe | ·ele hēṟiṁge 200 hasarakke ·aḍake 2 | ·iṁtī dharmmamaṁ pratipālisidavaru gaṁgevāraṇāśi-
kurukṣētradalu sahasra kavileyaṁ kōḍuṁ koḷagumaṁ suvarṇnadiṁ pa[ṁ]caratna-

dalu kaṭṭisi caturvvēdapāragarappa sahasrabrāhmaṇarigubhayamukhiyaṁ koṭṭa phalamakku ||
i dharmmamanārānuvaḷidātanā gaṁgevāraṇāsikurukṣētra-

dalā kavileyuvanā brāhmaṇarumaṁ koṁda paṁcamahāpātakanakku || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā
yō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ ṣaṣṭhivvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāya-

tē krimi[ḥ*] || sāmānyōyaṁ dharmasētuṁ nṛpāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanīyō bhava-
dbhiḥ sarbbānētānbhāvinaḥ pārtthīvēṁdrānbhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ ||
maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 344.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1904.)
ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE OF THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE
AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a composite record registering various gifts made at different periods. The
first is dated the cyclie year Sarvadhāri, Pushya 30, Brihaspativāra, Sūrya-grahana in
the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Narasiṅgadēva. The details work out quite correctly
for A.D. 1228 December 28, Thursday when there was a solar eclipse. The
Narasiṁhadēva of the record must, therefore, be Narasiṁha II. A certain Bommayya-
Daṇṇāyaka is stated to have made a gift of some land for the service of the god
Kalidēva of Bāguḷi.

The second is dated Sarvajit, Vaiśākha, ba. 10, Guruvāra corresponding to A.D.
1287 May 8, Thursday. The fifty (Mahājanas) of Bāguḷi are stated to have made a
gift of land for the service of the god Bidirahaḷḷi-Svāmidēva. In the third, some
land is said to have been granted to Vishṇughaisar of Gorūr in Khara, Chaitra śu.
7, Guruvāra = A.D. 1291 March 8, Thursday. In the fourth a certain Heggaḍe
(name lost) is said to have granted a portion of the toll-revenue for the service of the
god Kalinātha on Sunday. Bhādrapada ba. 30 of Āṅgirasa solar eclipse, probably =
A.D. 1332 September 20 Sunday. But on that day there was no solar eclipse. In the
fifth, merchants of various countries are said to have granted one tāra of betel-leaves
out of every naḍe for the service of the same god in Nandana, Vaiśākha, śu. 15,
Thursday 5 probably = A.D. 1292 May 1, Thursday; f.d.t. 87. In the sixth, the
Brāhmans are stated to have been exempted from sāmya • • • and vritti-
vyāhāra in Sarvajit, Āshādha, śu. 5, Sōmavāra, probably = A.D. 1287 June 17
Wednesday (the week day being an error). The seventh records a gift in Khara,
Mārgaśira, ba. 12 (not verifiable).

inscription.head:
No. 345.
(A.R. No. 126-A of 1913.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE VENUGOPALASVAMI TEMPLE AT MAGALA,
HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This record is engraved in continuation of No. 333. On the 10th day of Āśvayuja
of the cyclic year Vijaya (= probably A.D. 1233-34) Mahāvaḍḍavyavahāri Vāsudēva-
Nāyaka is said to have made a gift of 2 gadyāṇas for making a flower garden for the
service of the god Gōpāladēva.

l.ka: vijayasaṁvatsarada ·āsvayija su 10 daṁdu śrīmadanādiyagrahāraṁ māgoḷada yiṁ-
nūrvvara samakṣadalu śrīmanumahāvaḍḍabyavahāri vāsudēvanāykaru śrī gō-

pāḷadēvara hūdōṭakke gadyāṇaṁ ·eraḍanu būtara kallayana mēle vokalugaḍanāgi
koṭṭa-
ru hūgiḍuvanikki puṟpavanu dēvarige naḍasuva yī dharmmavanu yiṁnūrvvaru pālisu-

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1904.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE BASTI AT KOGALI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Yuva, Āśvayuja, śu. Paurṇamī, Guruvāra. Yuva in
Rāmanāthadēva's reign to which the record belongs was A.D. 1275. In this year
Āśvayuja śu. 15 fell on Sunday (not Thursday) 6th October. In the next year
Dhātu = A.D. 1276, the tithi did fall on Thursday 24th September. This was probably
the intended date. It registers the money-grants made by several Jaina devotees for
the daily ablusion of Chenna-Pārśvadēva of Kōgaḷi which is called a tīrtha.
inscription.h4:
First Face. The following two lines are engraved above this inscription:—
śrīmatkollāpurasthānē śrīsāmaṁtajināḷayē śrīmatkanakanaṁ-
dīti khyātācāryyō bhavammaniḥ || tacciṣya śrīprabhāca

l.ka: kōgiḷiya tīrtthada ceṁnapārśvadēvarige śrīmannāḷva prabhu dēvi-
māi seṭṭiya mādave ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ma 5
koḷḷipākiya sōyiseṭṭiya maga bo(ṁ)mmiseṭṭi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ga 1
koṭṭūra chaladaṁka baiciseṭṭiyaru nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa paṇa 5
koṭṭūra vaḍḍabevahāri kammadēva nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa gadyāna 1
koṭṭūra vaḍḍabevahāri vāsudēva nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa gadyāna 1
hoḷaguṁdeya sāvatta mallagauḍana maga kallagauḍa nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa gadyāna 1
hoḷaguṁdeya māriseṭiya cikkarāmayya nityābhisēkake koṭṭa ma 5
jeṁtole sirivura jainakāḷayya nityābhisēkhake koṭṭa gadyāna 1
kaḍaṁba nāgayyana magaḷu banadauve nityābhisēkake koṭṭa ma 5
koṭūra beṭṭiseṭiya rāmayya nityābhisēkake koṭṭa ma 5
koṭūra kētiseṭṭiya maga cava[ḍa]ya nityābisēkake koṭṭa gadyāna 1
koṭūra heggaḍe māidēvana maga vāmaya nityābhisēkake koṭṭa gadyāna 1
tippaṇana kumāri cokaladēvi ·akṣa[ya*]bhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkake koṭṭa ga 1

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1904.)
ON ANOTHER PILLAR IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Dhātu, Āśvayuja, śu. pūrṇimā, Guruvāra correspond-
ing to A.D. 1276 September 24 Thursday. It is therefore apparent that Yuva in the

2. Lines 33 and 34 are written at the top of the first face and line 35 at the top of the second.
previous record is a mistake for Dhātu. This also refers itself to the reign of the
Hoysaḷa king Rāmanāthadēva and records the gifts of money made by several
devotees (named) to the temple of Chenna-Pārśvadēva at Kōgaḷi.

l.ka: namaḥ siddhēbhyaḥ svasti śrīmatu yādavanārāyaṇa pratāpacakravartti śrīvīrarāma-
nāthadēva sthirarājyaṁgeyutirdda dhātusaṁvatsarada ·āśvayija śuddha paurṇnami guru-
hiriyaboṁmayana madavaḷige kāmavve ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ma 5
vuccaṁgiya kāḷiseṭṭiya maga siripaya ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ga 1
vuccaṁgiya kāḷiseṭṭiya maga gōpaya ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ga 1
kaṁmāṟa kētōjana maga basavōja ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ga 1
nāvida caṭṭōjana maga sōvōja ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ga 1
vaṇigeya māgara kētaiya ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa haṇa ma 5
nāciya kāḷiya maga heggaḍe ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nitya ·abisēkha ga 1
sēnabova kēsavadēvana maga maidēvanu ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ga 1
sēnabōva virupaya ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa paṇa ma 5
mādigavuḍana maga māragavuḍa ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ga 1
hegaḍe basavayana maga nāgiseṭṭi ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa va 5
balakiseṭṭiya maga nāgaya ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ma 5
kētaya dēvaiyanu ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa haṇa ma 5
bōgāṟa cauvuḍaiyana heṁḍati māidēvi ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi koṭṭa ga 1
sēnabhōva tikaiyana maga sōvaṇna ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nitya·abhisēkakke koṭṭa ma
meḷḷase sōmaiyana maga dēviseṭṭi ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nitya·abhisēkakke koṭṭa ma 5
moṭeya muddayana maga cauḍiseṭṭi ·akṣayabhaṁḍāravāgi nityābhisēkakke koṭṭa ga 1
kattiya maduhayana ta(ṁ)mma bomaya nityābhisēkhake koṭṭa 5 ma [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(A.R. No. 748 of 1909.)
ON THE WEST WALL OF THE KITCHEN OF THE MUKTESVARA TEMPLE IN
SAMAYAVARAM, LALGUDI TALUK, TRICHINOPOLY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and refers to the Hoysaḷa king Rāmanāthadēva. It mentions a
certain Haritiya-Nāyaka, son of Padumaya-Nāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1918.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE FRONT MANDAPA OF THE PAMPAPATI TEMPLE AT
BENNEHALLI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the cyclic year Chitrabhānu, Bhādrapada, ba. 8, Bṛihavāra in the
reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Narasiṁhadēva. Narasiṁha I ruled from A.D. 1159
to 1173 and 1162 corresponded to Chitrabhānu. In that year Bhādrapada ba. 8 was
Tuesday, but not Thursday. In the reign of Narasiṁha II there was no Chitra-
bhānu. In the reign of Narasiṁha III (A.D. 1254 to 1292) Chitrabhānu coincided
with A.D. 1282. In this year Bhādrapada ba. 8 was Thursday, 27th August. On
palaeographic grounds also the record may be placed in the 13th century. So the
king referred to here was probably Narasiṁha III. A certain Giripayya-Piriyaṇṇa
is stated to have made a gift of a cow and a buffalow for the service of the god
Virūpākshadēva of Beṇṇevūru.

l.ka: mahājanaṁgaḷa bhōjanakkaṁ biṭṭa nalleṁme nallāvu kōgaḷināḍa prabhu-
ya hadikeyanettuva heggaḍegaḷāru baṁdaḍaṁ taṁma dharmavāgi pra-

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1918.)
ON THE SAME PILLAR. This inscription is written in continuation of No.
inscription.p:
This is dated Chitrabhānu, Bhādrapada, amāvāse, Sōmavāra in the reign of the
Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Narasiṁha (III). The date is irregular. In Chitrabhānu = A.D.
1282, Bhādrapada amāvāsyā fell on Thursday, 6th September. A number of persons
(named), along with Masaṇeya-Sāvanta, are said to have made a gift for the
service of the same god as the one mentioned in No. 349 above and for feeding 12
Brāhmans.

l.ka: ggaḍemaneya naṁnabōvana maga ·ayabōva cigaṭēṟa caṁdabōva kuttaṁ-
giya saṁgabōvana rāmabōva hoḷaguṁdeya guṁḍabōva kō-
vāgi śrīmatu beṁṇevūrastaḷadalu mahānāḍāgi neredu ·ā vūra
mma biluvaḍike mukhyavāgi kabbilavaḍike ·āvudu baṁdaḍaṁ sarvvanama-

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(A.R. No. 105-A of 1904.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE MALE-MALLAPPA TEMPLE
AT BAGALI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated in the year Nandana, Kārtika, śu. 5 Sōmavāra in the
reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Narasiṁha (III). If panchamī is a mistake for
paurṇamī the date would correspond to A.D. 1292 October 27 Monday. The Heggaḍe
Chandayya, son of Beṭṭiya-Nāyaka, who was the Haṟekāra of Moguvāḍa-Mācharasa
made a gift of some garden land for the service of the god Nīlēśvaradēva.

l.ka: nasaṁvatsarada kārtika sudda paṁchami sōmavāradaṁdu śrīmatu ha-
nu heggaḍe caṁdayyanu ·ā dēvara ·aṁgabhōgaraṁgabhōgakke beḷḷeyakaṭṭada tanna

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(A.R. No. 116-A of 1913.)
ON THE FOURTH SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD THE BHIMESVARA TEMPLE
AT NILAGUNDA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This record, which is engraved in continuation of No. 340, registers the gift of the
village Talavāgilahaḷḷi by the Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara Bijjarasa-Achchutadēva. It is dated
the cyclic year Kīlaka, Phālguṇa, śu. 13, Ādityavāra = probably A.D. 1309 February
25, Sunday, the only date for which the details work out correctly during the 13th
and 14th centuries to which period the record may be assigned palaeographically.

l.ka: ṁneya kṣētrasā(ṁ)mya sahita 80 koṁḍu śrīhariharadēvara muṁde dāna-
krayavāgi dhārāpūrvvaka māḍi koṭṭaru || maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(A.R. No. 738 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT ALUDI, MADAKSIRA TALUK,
ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1238, Naḷa, Phālguṇa, śu. 5, Guruvāra, corresponding
to A.D. 1317 February 17, Thursday, when Mahāmaṇḍalēsvara Tiparasa,
stated to be the son of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷadēva (III), and Bhairavadēvarasa
granted land at ? Hāluviḍi as koḍagi to Masaṇaseṭṭi of Kariyamaṟe, who was the head
of merchants.

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(A.R. No. 772 of 1917.)
ON TWO PIECES OF A BROKEN HERO-STONE LYING NEAR THE ENTRANCE INTO THE
VILLAGE SIRIVARA, HINDUPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1243, Raudri, Bhādrapada, ba. 13, Sōmavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1320 September 1, Monday in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷadēva
(III). It records the death of the Mahāsāmantādhipati Hiriya Bommeya-Nāyaka, son
of Gaṅgeya-Nāyaka of Hoḷakallu in a fight against the cavalry of the chief (Manneya)
of Mēlumāvu, when Aḷiya Mācheya-Daṇṇāyaka, the prime minister of the king was
ruling at Penugoṇḍe. It records the gift of wet and garden lands beneath the
Sirivara tank to those who were appointed to worship the hero-stone.

l.ka: svasti [||*] śrīmatu sakavarsa 1243 raudrisaṁvatsarada bhādrapada ba 13 sō | vīraballā-
nāykaru kudureya daḷadamēle ·ēṟi hoyikuṭṭadi raṇadalu biddu svargastarā-

śrīmatu ·i bīrakallapratiṣṭheya mūruvali pūjepunaskāra māḍuva sthanikarige sirivarada keṟe-
ya hiṁ-

de gadde ko 5 keyi ko 5 sarbbabhādhe parihāravāgi ·ā māceyada(ṁ)ṇnāykaru dhārā-
pūrbba-
kavāgi koṭṭa datti maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(A.R. No. 796 of 1917.)
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR A WELL TO THE NORTH OF TAUNEPALLI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1245, Rudhirōdgāri, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 5, probably corresponding to
A.D. 1323 July 9 Saturday (not verifiable) in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-
Ballāḷadēvarasa (III). His Mahāpradhāna Aḷiya Mācheya-Daṇṇāyaka made a gift of
land in Maḍuvakūṇṭeyahaḷḷi in Ṟāmaḍi-nāḍu to Maliṟaḍi, son of Bommiṟaḍi of Būdali
as a koḍagi. The koḍagi was handed over to the donee by Bommaṇṇa and Dēvaṇṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(A.R. No. 771 of 1917.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP NEAR THE ENTRANCE INTO THE VILLAGE,
SIRIVARAM, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1246, Rudhirōdgāri, Chaitra, śu. 10, Sōmavāra corresponding
to A.D. 1324 March 5, Monday. The cyclic year was, however, Raktākshi not Rudhi-
rōdgāri. The inscription refers itself to the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa-
dēva (III), whose Mahāpradhāna Aḷiya Mācheya-Dannāyaka is stated here to have
gained a victory over Rācheya-Nāyaka of Tuṁbeyakallu. It records the death of
Ajapa, son of Dhīmagōḍa of Sirivara in the fight. The record is a little damaged.

l.ka:
ru geddalli śrimanumahānāḍapra[bhu] sirivarada dhīmagōḍana maga ·ajapanu voḍeyara • •

[||*]

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1912.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF A PRIVATE HOUSE AT KODIGEPALLE,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1250, Vibhava, [Bhā]drapada, ba. 7, Ma[ndavāra ?]
corresponding to A.D. 1328 August 7, Saturday; f.d.t. ·36. It belongs to the reign
of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa (III), son of Vishṇuvardhana Pratāpacha[kravarti]
Vīra-Narasiṁhadēvarasa. The king's Mahāpradhāna, Mācheya-Daṇṇāyaka, who was
ruling at the nelevīḍu of Penugoṇḍe made a koḍagi gift of the village Beṇagūru to
Nāgeya-Gavuda and others. It is a little damaged.

l.note: Below this is written a Telugu inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(A.R. No. 780 of 1917.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT
SANJIVIRAYANIPALLI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1262, Vikrama, Bhādrapada, śu. 10, Va[ḍḍavāra] corresponding
to A.D. 1340 September 2, Saturday in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa-
dēvarasa (III). His Mahāpradhāna Īśvara-Daṇṇāyaka, son of Mācheya-Daṇṇāyaka,
made a gift of Valagalakuṇṭeyahaḷḷi as a koḍagi to Gōpa-Gavuṇḍa, son of Chavaka
Bomma-Gāvuṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(A.R. No. 102 of 1927.)
ON A ROCK NEAR THE TANK ON THE TOP OF THE HILL FORT AT KUNDURPI,
KALYANADRUG TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and is dated Śaka 1262, the other details being lost. It registers
the excavation of a tank called Haṁpeya-Doṇe in Kundurupe by Chikka Bommayya-
Nāyaka, son of Gaṅgeya-Nāyaka of Holakal, in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-
Ballāḷa (III), son of Vīra-Narasiṅga, ruling from Dōrasamudra. Bommaya-Nāyaka is
stated to have been governing Nidugalla-rājya.

l.ka: daṁḍugalaki sitagaragaṁḍa maṟehokkaḍekāvaṁ hoḷakala gaṁgeyanāyakara kumāra cikka
ḍeyalu hostāgi haṁpeya doṇeya tōḍsi barasida śāsana maṁgaḷa mahā-

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(A.R. No. 453 of 1914.)
ON A FRAGMENT LYING BEFORE VIRAPPA'S HOUSE AT SOGI,
HADAGALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is very much damaged and the date is lost. It belongs to the reign of the
Hoysaḷa king Vishṇuvardhana Vīra-Ballāḷa. There is no means of ascertaining
whether he was the II, III or IV of that name. Tilakayya, son of Kāḷimayya who
himself was a descendant of Nāgavarma's family is said to have granted some land to
a Jaina teacher named Ubhayāchāryya of Kōgaḷi.

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(A.R. No. 197 of 1918.)
ON A STONE OIL-MILL SET UP IN A FIELD NEAR TAGARAPURAM,
KOLLEGAL TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This records the erection (?) of the oilmill by certain persons, named in the
inscription, during the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷa. The date Bhāva,
Vaiśākha, ba. 9 is not verifiable.

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(A.R. No. 220-A of 1918.)
Below No. 325.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and its date is lost. Vīra-Pāṇḍya seems to have made some
grant for the service of the god Āhavamalladēva. As the praśasti resembles that of
the Hoysaḷas, this may be a Hoysaḷa record.

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(A.R. No. 552 of 1915.)
ON A BROKEN STONE LYING NEAR THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT PEDDAKOTTALIKI,
ADONI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is very much damaged and dated Śaka 1125, Dundubhi, Pushya, śu. [8],
Bṛihavāra. The date seems to be irregular. Pushya śu. 8 in Dundubhi ended on
Tuesday = 24th December A.D. 1202. On Thursday the tithi was śu. 11, which was
also the day of Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti (= A.D. 1202 December 26). This was
probably the day intended. The inscription refers itself to the reign of the Yādava
king Siṅgaṇa. It records that the king's minister Basavarasa, who was ruling over
Sindavāḍi one-thousand, made a gift of some plots of land for the service of the god
Amritēśvara-Gōpinātha and Chenna-Sōmanāthadēva and also to several Brāhmans
(named).

vige gāṇa 1 nānādēsi muṁmuri[daṁḍaṁ]gaḷu koṭṭāya hoṁge • • • koṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(A.R. No. 498 of 1915.)
ON A STONE LYING IN A FIELD NEAR DEVARABETTA, SAME
TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the 12th year of the reign of the Yādava king Siṅgaṇa-
dēva, Pramōdūta, Māgha, śu. 2, Ādivāra. In Pramōdūta, Māgha śu. 2 fell on Tuesday
(not Sunday) 18th January A.D. 1211. If, however, Māgha is a mistake for Pausha
the date would be equivalent to A.D. 1210 December 19 Sunday. It records the gift of
the toll-revenue of Moladakallu by Bhilluva-Nāyaka, who was in charge of the tolls of
Sindavāḍi one-thousand in the manne of Jagadāḷa Sōmaya-Nāyaka, for the service
of the god Mallikārjuna.

l.ka: ka taḷa sārige hoṟa • • • • voḷage suṁ-
ḍidaru maṁgaḷa mahāśrī | sudattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(A.R. No. 345 of 1920.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD TO THE NORTH OF THE VILLAGE, NILURU,
GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the 6th year of the reign of the Yādava king Siṁhaṇa-
dēva, Śaka 1137, Yuva, Vaiśākha, śu. 10, Bṛihavāra corresponding to A.D. 1215 April
10 Friday, not Thursday. It records that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jagatāpu Daṇḍidēva-
Chōḷamahārāya, son of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Madhurāntakadēma-Chōḷamahārāja,
made a gift of some plots of land for the service of god Bhōgēśvaradēva and for the
subsistence of the temple servants, when he was ruling at the nelevīḍu of Hāṁbuḷige.
The writer of the praśasti was Sēnabōva Bācharasa.

l.ka: ḷida dōṣavaku || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā ·ō ha-

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(A.R. No. 298 of 1926.)
ON A BLACK STONE PLANTED IN THE COMPOUND OF THE TEMPLE OF
CHENNAKESAVASVAMI AT MADHURAPURAM, A DEPOPULATED HAMLET OF
DAMPETLA, DHARMAVARAM TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1147, Tāraṇa, Māgha, śu. 11, Maṅgaḷavāra corresponding to
A.D. 1225 January 21 Tuesday, in the reign of the Yādava king Siṁhaḷadēva. It
records that Jaya • • •, son of Dhannayya-Rāhuta, made a grant of land to the
Mahājanas of Madhurāpura for the service of the god Alahaperumāludēva of the
village.

l.ka: yu tiṁdavaru maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā vō harēti vasu-

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(A.R. No. 520 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB LYING NEAR THE TANK-BUND AT CHINNA-TUMBALAM,
ADONI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1[15]1, Virōdhi, Chaitra, śu. 10, Budhavāra. “In
A.D. 1229, Chaitra (lunar) su. 10 fell on Tuesday. But in the solar month of Chittirai
or Mēsha, śu. 10 commenced on Wednesday, 4th April = (11 Mesha) at ·06 and ended at
·04 on the following day. The month of Chaitra in the inscription may perhaps have
been meant for the solar month Chittirai or Mēsha”. The record mentions Murāri
Kēśava, the minister of the Yādava king Jaitugi as the lord of the dēśa (probably
the Sindhuvāḍi-vishaya). It records that Anantapriya, disciple of Ananta, who was a
disciple of Jñānapriya, set up the god Yōga-Narasiṁhadēva to the north of the tank
(made by ?) Joyya and situated to the west of the village Tuṁbuḷa, which was at a
distance of a yōjana from the river Tuṅgabhadrā and which was located in the Sindhu-
vāḍī-vishaya. The Mahājanas of the village are stated to have made a gift of some
plots of land for the service of the god mentioned. It also registers other grants
of land and tolls made for the service of the same god.

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(A.R. No. 84 of 1904.)
ON THE THIRD PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE NARASIMHASVAMI SHRINE IN THE
KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This record belongs to the reign of the Yādava king Kandharadēva and is dated
in the 5th year of his reign, the cyclic year Sādhāraṇa, Pushya, ba. 30, Vyatīpāta and
Śravaṇa, corresponding to A.D. 1250 January 4 on which day Uttarāshāḍha
was current till ·53 of the day and Śravaṇa began after that. It registers the gift
of Siṅganahāḷu to the Mahājanas of Bāguḷi by Jannamarasa under the orders
Chavuḍiseṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 369.
(A.R. No. 732 of 1919.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE ISVARA TEMPLE AT NAGARURU, ALUR
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1176, Ānanda, Āshāḍha, śu. 11, Sōmavāra, corresponding to
A.D. 1254 June 28 Sunday, not Monday, as stated. It belongs to the reign of the

1. The right side of the slab is broken away and lost.
Yādava king Kannaradēva. His Mahāpradhāna Jōgama-Rāhuta, ruling over Sindavādi-
nāḍu is said to have made a gift of some sotra (land) for god Sōmanāthadēva's satra
(feeding house).

l.ka: pūrvakavāgi dēvara sarvvamānya chatra ga 5 yikkuva-

inscription.head:
No. 370.
(A.R. No. 733 of 1919.)
ON THE SAME SLAB.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1177, Rākshasa, Āshāḍha, śu. 11, Sōmavāra. The date is
irregular; śu. 11 of Āshāḍha in Rākshasa corresponded to A.D. 1255 June 17
Thursday, not Monday, in the reign of the Yādava king Kanharadēva. Kaṁhā-
rashaṇaṅgidēva, the chief of Mahāghāṭa, is said to have made a grant of land
at Nagarūru to the chhatra (feeding house) attached to the temple of the god
Sōmanātha.

l.ka: prajegaḷa muṁde koṭa sarvva[mā]nya maṁgaḷa mā-

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(A.R. No. 525 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT YENIGI,
HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is a little damaged and dated the 13th year of the reign of the Yādava king
Kandharadēva, Śaka 1181, Kāḷayukta, Pushya, Amāvāse, Sōmavāra, Uttarāyaṇa,-saṅka-
rānti, Sūrya-grahaṇa, Vyatīpāta. “There is a curious error in this date. In Kāḷayukti
(= A.D. 1258-59, Śaka 1181 current) the month Pausha was Kshaya i.e., suppressed
and there could have been no Pushya Amāvāsyā in that year, nor did any Amāvāsyā
that could possibly correspond to ordinary Pushya Amāvāsyā fall in that year on
Monday.” It records that, while the king was camping at the nelevīḍu of Dēvagiri,
the 120 Brāhman Mahājanas of Pūvina-Paḍaṅgile, who are described as very
learned in the Vēdas and the Śāstras, performing Aupāsana and Agnihōtra and as
“ripukula-kadalī-vana-kuñjarar and śaraṇāgata-vajra-pañjarar,” that is to say, des-
tructive to enemies as elephants to the forest of plantain-trees, and mail-armour to
those who seek shelter under them, made a gift of various plots of land of specified
boundary, for the service of the god Kusmanātha at the village. Pūvina-Paḍaṅgile
is stated to have been situated in the Kōgaḷi-nāḍu, which was the eye of the Noḷamba-
vāḍi-nāḍu which again was, as it were, the nose of Kuntala-dēśa. It is no wonder
that, as required by the times, the Brāhmans of those days were ready to take
up arms.

·uramuṁdaṇa haṁpanakeṟege hōda dāriyiṁ teṁkalu mallikārjunadēvara hūdōṁṭadiṁ paḍa-

tōṁṭadiṁ paḍuvalu

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(A.R. No. 446 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT
KOTNAKALLU, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1185, Dundubhi, Kārtika, śu. 15, Sōmavāra, vyatīpāta, Saṅkrā-
nti, grahaṇa. In Dundubhi, Kārttika śu. 15 corresponded to A.D. 1262 October 28
Saturday, not Monday, Vṛiśchika-Saṅkrānti; but on that day there was no lunar eclipse.
The inscription refers itself to the reign of the Yādava king Mahādēvarāya ruling from
Dēvagiri. It records that a certain Maduvaya of Kōṭiganūru obtained five mattars of
land as a gift from the five hundred Mahājanas of the village Kauravagrāma, known
as southern Ayyāvoḷe in Māsavāḍi one-hundred and forty, and made a gift of the
same for the service of the god Kōṭiśaṅkaradēva on the right bank of the Tuṅga-
bhadrā river. Chāvōja, son of Indōja, engraved this.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] śrīmatu samastapraśastisahitaṁ śrīsēvaṇamahādēvarāyavijeyarājyada śakavarṣa
1185 neya duṁdubhisaṁvatsarado-

ḷu dēvagiryya nelevīḍinoḷu sukhasaṁkathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyutta(ṁ)mire svasti
śrīmatu samastapraśastisahitaṁ māsavāḍi nū-

ṟanālvatakaṁ śirōmaṇiyenisida dakṣiṇa·aiyāvaḷe ·enippa kauravagrāmadūroḍeyapramu-
khyavāgainūrvvaru dakṣiṇavāra-

ṇāśiyanippa tuṁgabhadreya dakṣiṇatīradalu nelasi[da] svayaṁbhuśrīkōṭiśa[ṁ*]kharadēvarige
ā saṁvatsa[ra*]da kārtika śu 15 sō-

mavāra byatīpāta saṁkrāṁti grahaṇada ·aga(ṁ)ṇyapu(ṁ)ṇyatithiyalu kōṭiganūra maduvainu
a ·ainūrvvara kaiyyalu

taru 5 śrīśaṁkhara

kaṁḍarisidāta

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(A.R. No. 488 of 1914.)
ON A NANDI-PILLAR LYING IN A FIELD TO THE SOUTH-EAST OF THE VILLAGE
HYARADA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1184, Dundubhi, Chaitra, ba. amāvāse, Sōmavāra,
Sūrya-grahaṇa. The date intended was probably A.D. 1263 April 9 Monday; the cyclic
year was Rudhirōdgārin and there was no eclipse. The record belongs to the reign of
the Yādava king Mahādēvarāya and seems to record that the Mahāpasāyita Daḷavayya
• • • • Sōyidēva made a gift of the village Heraḍa for the service of the god
Mallikārjunadēva at Śrīparvata. The geographical division Beṇṇevūru twelve is
mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(A.R. No. 519 of 1914.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT CHIMNAHALLI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the 6th year of the reign of the Yādava king Vīra-Mahādēva, cyclic
year Krōdhana, Ashādha, ba. amāvāse, [Sō]mavāra, corresponding to A.D. 1265 July
13 Monday; f.d.t. • 33. It records that Sōyidēvarasa made a gift of the village
Nelkudure.

l.note: This is engraved in continuation of No 88.

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1925.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE HALL BEFORE THE GARBHAGRIHA OF THE SIDDHALINGESVARA
TEMPLE AT UJJINI, KUDLIGI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the 9th year of the reign of the Yādava king Mahā-
dēvarāya, cyclic year Vibhava, Kārtika, ba • • • • • Sūrya-grahaṇa corresponding
to A.D. 1268 November 6 Tuesday, when there was a solar eclipse. It records the
gift of a mānya land for the service of some god (name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(A.R. No. 717 of 1919.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE KALLARAYYA TEMPLE AT NERANIKI,
ALUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1198, the 5th year of the reign of the Yādava king Rāma-
chandradēva, cyclic year Dhātu, Chaitra, śu. 1, Maṅgaḷavāra corresponding to A.D.
1276 March 17 Tuesday; f.d.t. 18. It records that Lahkumidēva-Nāyaka, agent of
Vasudēva, made a grant of 4 mattars of land for the service of the god Kalinātha-
dēva. It also specifies the various articles of offerings to be made to the god.

l.ka: saṁvaścarada caitra su 1 maṁ vadalu vasudēvana
mānyāgi bha dēvara paḍi hotare ·obaḷa ·aki mose-
de koḷaga hattaṟa bede vāsadē[va*]na lakhumidē-

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(A.R. No. 478 of 1915.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT
BASSARAKODU, ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1200, Bahudhānya, Jyēshṭha, śu. 1, Guruvāra. The date is not
regular; in Bahudhānya, Jēshṭa śu. 1 corresponded to A.D. 1278 May 24 Tuesday,
not Thursday as stated. The record refers itself to in the reign of the Yādava king
Rāmachandradēva ruling from Dēvagiri. The Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kavitaḍaka Bhīma-
dēva-Rāṇeyya, who was ruling over Ādavani etc. in Sindavāḍi, made a sarvamānya gift
of Basurukōḍu to the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Bommarasa, son of Nāgarasa.

l.ka: • • vāgi koṭṭanu maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī
svadattaṁ paradattāṁ vā yō narēta vasuṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1918.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE ANJANEYA TEMPLE AT KUNCHURU, HARAPANAHALLI
TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the 10th year of the Yādava king Rāmachandradēva, cyclic year
Pramāthi, Āshāḍha, ba. 10, Sōmavāra. In Pramāthi = A.D. 1279, Āshāḍh ba.
10 fell on July 5 Wednesday, not Monday; Sōmavara is probably a mistake
for Saumyavāra. The inscription records that the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara, Nava-
khaṇḍeya-chakravarti Kheyidēva-Rāṇeya, also called Kandhāradēva, together with
Jagaddaḷa Nāchidēvarasa, who was the Manneya of Haluvāgilu, Malliya, the Ūroḍeya
of the village Koṁchūru and other Mānyakāras and people made a grant of a srotra
land for the service of the god Svayambhu-Kalinātha. It states that the violator of
the charity would be excommunicated from the eighteen samayas.

l.ka: yanuṁ brāhmaṇananuṁ vadhiyisida phala yidanu vo-
ge hōgi narakake bīḷuvaru || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō ha-
śrī śrī śrī || mate yo staḷadali yāvananu ·a-

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1918.)
ON A PILLAR IN THE CENTRAL MANDAPA OF THE PAMPAPATI TEMPLE AT
BENNEHALLI, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the 10th year of the reign of the Yādava king Rāmachandradēva,
cyclic year Pramāthi, Phālguṇa, śu. 11, Sōmavāra corresponding to A.D. 1280 February
12, Monday. It records the grant by the Rājaguru Vubhaṇayitavāhi-Dēvarāṇeya of
gadyāṇas 8 to the 12 Mahājanas of Beṇṇeyahaḷḷi and 2 mattars to Kalajīya on account
of the charity of Ramābāyi for a water-shed in the samthe.

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(A.R. No. 224 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT HALUVAGALU,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1204, Chitrabhānu, Vaiśākha, śu. 14, Sōmavāra,
Svāti-nakshatra and Vyatīpāta. In Chitrabhānu, Vaiśākha śu. 14 ended on Thursday
which was combined with Svāti and Vyatīpāta-yōga; = A.D. 1282 April 23;
the week day is an error. The record belongs to the reign of the Yādava king Rāma-
chandrarāya, who is described as the rescuer of the earth from the depredations of
the Turushka, the conqueror of the Hoysaḷa sovereignty etc. It registers the grant
of a plot of land at Haluvāgalu to the Sthānika Jōgarāśi for the service of the god
Kalinātha in the village made by Vāsudēva, the chief of the village, Nācharasa,
the eight Hiṭṭu and other people. The gift was made under the orders of Pavasariya
Haridēva, the Adhikhāri of Pāṇḍya-nāḍu and a subordinate of the Sainyādhipati
Mahāpradhāna Kannaradēva, who was governing all the dēśas including Huligeṟe.
It also records gifts made by the Nakharas and Mummuridaṇḍas for the same god.

l.ka: ri kannaradēva huligeṟe mukhyavāgi palavuṁ dēśaṁgaḷa(ṁ)nēkādhiṣṭitavāgi vi-
bhu mūlasthānakallināthadēvara tīrthakṣētra[da*]llina viśēṣamaṁ kēḷdu ·ā dēvara pūrvvaśo-
tramaṁ sarvvanamasyavāgi biṭṭu taṁma pāṁḍyanāḍha ·adhikāri pavasari-
nācara[sara] parivāra ·eṁṭuhiṭṭu paṁcamaṭha samasta prajegaḷu mukhyavā-
gi | śakavarṣa 1204 neya citrabhānusaṁvaṁtsa[ra*]da vaiśākha su 14 sōma-
jaguru jōgarāsige dhārāpūrvvakadi śrīkalidēvargge śotramaṁ sarvvana-
masyavāgi biṭṭaru || ḥ || haluvāgila paṁneraḍaṟalliyāṟu kai gadde
ykanu dēvarige voṁdu • • • • koṭṭa ·ā puradoḷage • voṁdu gāṇamaṁ
dēvarige naṁdādīvigege • • • svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yō harēti va-
suṁdharā[ṁ*] ṣaṣṭivarṣasaha[srāṇāṁ] viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ || priya-
yidaṁ kāyade kāyva pāpige (||) kurukṣētraṁgaḷoḷu vāraṇā-

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1918.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP NEAR THE ISVARA TEMPLE AT KADATI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the 13th year of the reign of the Yādava king Rāma-
chandradēva, cyclic year Chitrabhānu, Vaiśākha, ba. 2, Ādivāra corresponding to A.D.
1282 April 26 Sunday. It seems to record the death of a hero in a battle waged by
Basavadēva, (son of ?) Bayirarsadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(A.R. No. 486 of 1914.)
ON THE BASE OF THE PILLAR OF THE MUKHA-MANDAPA OF THE KALLESVARA
TEMPLE AT MAILARA, HADAGALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1205, Chitrabhānu, Jyēshṭha śu. 1, • •
corresponding to A.D. 1282 May 9 Saturday not verifiable), in the reign of the Yādava
king Vīra-Rāmachandra. It records that Jannuva-Nāyaka set up Nandikēśvara before
the god Kalināthadēva at Guṅguṟāchanda.

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1918.)
ON A PILLAR OF THE MALLIKARJUNASVAMI TEMPLE AT KUNCHURU,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the 15th year of the reign of the Yādava king Vīra-Rāmachandra-
rāya, cyclic year Sarvajit, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, Sōmavāra. In Sarvajit = 1287 the tithi
cited fell on Saturday, not Monday as stated. But in the previous year it fell on
Monday = A.D. 1286 August 5, which was probably the intended date. The
inscription records that under the orders of Liṅgadēva-Rāṇeya, son of Maṇḍalika
Jājjigidēva, Sarvādhikāri Jakkarasa made over the sotra (land) of the god Malli-
kārjunadēva of the village Mattavūra to the chief priest Kalleyajīya-Haripajīya,
in the presence of Jagadāḷa Nācharasa, Mallaṇṇa, the eight Hiṭṭus and other people.

l.ka:
śrīyādavanārāyaṇa bhujabaḷaprauḍhapratāpacakravarti śrīvīrarāmacaṁdrarāyavijaya-
rājyōdayada ha 15 neya sarbba-

jitusaṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa su 15 sōmavāradali bhārasaṁkaleya maṁḍaḷika jājjigidēvanaku-
māra liṁgadēvarāṇe-

yanu mattavūra mallikārjunadēvara śotravanu ·aṁgabhōga ·aṁritavaḍige liṁgadēvarāṇe-
yana nirūpadiṁ

śrīmatu sarvvādhikāri jakkarasaru śrīmaṁmahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ maṁneyagoḍati jagadāḷanācarsaru
vūraḍe-

ya malla(ṁ)ṇnamukhyavāda samastapraje ·eṇṭuhiṭṭu sthānamā(ṁ)nyada muṁde ·ā jakkarasanu
mallikārjunadēvara ·ācā-
ṇana kapileya ta(ṁ)nna svahastadiṁ koṁda brahmātidōsava-

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(A.R. No. 718 of 1919.)
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE SUNKALAMMA TEMPLE AT NERANIKI,
ALUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated the 17th year of the reign of the Yādava king Rāmachandradēva,
Śaka 1209, Śarvajitu, Srāvaṇa, śu. 15, Guruvāra corresponding to A.D. 1287 July
24 Friday, not Thursday, the 15th tithi commencing about midday on Friday.
It records that Dēva-Rāṇeya, who was administering (Sindavāḍi-nāḍu ?) granted
Nāgaya-Nāyaka, son of Srīkaṇṭha-Nāyaka, four villages viz., Nēralike, Beṁbuguḍi,
Arakeṟe and Hoḷalugunda as vṛitti and that the chief Seṭṭis of Tuṁbuḷapaṭṭaṇa
granted a cess of 2 paṇas on every load of ten bullocks to the Nāyaka.

l.ka: khyarappa seṭṭigutti ruddiseṭṭi savudore sōyiseṭṭi hega-
mārggada guḍḍa galiseṭṭiyara maga dēviseṭṭi[ya*]ru doḍā-
śnutē || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharā[ṁ]

l.note:
At the top of the inscription are written:
muliga kalappa samakṣavāgi

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1918.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP IN THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT HALUVAGILU,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated the 18th year of the reign of the Yādava king
Rāmachandradēva, cyclic year Sarvajitu (= A.D. 1287-88), other details being lost.
It seems to record the death of Seṁjiya-Mallaya with laurels of victory in a battle
field.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] śrīmatu yādavanārāyaṇaṁ bhujabaḷaprauḍhapratāpacakravartti śrīrāmacaṁdra
dēvarāya-

rājyōdayada 18 neya śarvvajitusaṁvatsarada • • • • • • dase sōmavā-
radaṁ-

kādi geluda ·āneyida seṁjiyamallaya • • • • • • • • • • kādi gelidu
suralō-

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1918.)
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP IN THE ISVARA TEMPLE AT KADATI,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and dated Śaka 1214, Nandana (= A.D. 1292-93), Phālguṇa, in
the reign of the Yādava king Vīra-Rāmachandrarāya. It records that the Manneya-
goḍiti Chaṭṭarasa fought at the command of Kandharadēva, and died after beheading
a number of enemies.

l.ka: • • • • hokkara kāva śaraṇāgatavajrapanjaraṁ birudamaṁneyara gaṁḍa śrīsō

svāmikāryake taḷteṟedu kādi birddu svargava sūṟegoṁḍa bīragalla maṁgaḷa mahā-
śrī [||*]
[kaṁ]dharana caraṇa śaraṇeṁdu khaṁḍeyamaṁ koṁḍu niṁdu mārvalamellamaṁ ta[ṟi]-
du ripugaḷa taleyaṁ koṁḍārdu niṁda maṁneyacaṭṭaṁ | jitēna labhyatē lakṣmī[r*]mritē-

[nāpi] surāṁganā | kṣaṇavidhvaṁsanīyē kāyē kā ciṁtā maraṇē raṇē || sivasūtradhāri
sosagōjana ma-

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(A.R. No. 256 of 1918.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE SITE OF A RUINED MATHA AT MANNERA-
MASALAVADA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1219, Hēmaḷambi, Mārgaśira, śu. 5, Guruvāra corresponding
to A.D. 1297 November 20, Wednesday, not Thursday, in the reign of the Yādava
king Rāmachandradēva. It records a gift of some plots of land to Vinayachandradēva,
disciple of Nēmichandra-Rāvuḷa of the Postaka-gachchha, Dēśi-gaṇa and Mūla-saṅgha,
by the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bhairavadēvarasa, a Vaishṇava in faith, with the Gauḍas
and others for the Jina temple at Mosaḷevāḍa, which was repaired by Kēśava-Paṇḍita,
son of Sāvanta-Paṇḍita, the prime-minister of the Mahamaṇḍalēśvara Sāḷiveya
Tīkamadēva-Rāṇeya. It also records the grant of some other plots of land by the
eight Hiṭṭus and the people of the village, with Pārisa-Gauḍa of Hagaranūru at
their head, to the temple of Pārśvanāthadēva constructed by Kannāyi at Moasaḷa-
vāḍa. Kannāyi is stated to be the mother of Kēśava-Paṇḍita.

vūriṁdaṁ mūḍaṇa deseyalu taraḷamāreyanāykana keyiṁ baḍagalu kaliseṭṭi māḍu-

va kavuṁgina keyiṁ mūḍalu kavaḍeya dēviseṭṭi māḍuva keyiṁ paḍuvalu ·ā kavaḍeya sā-
yise-

ṭṭi māḍuva keyiṁ teṁkalu mattaru yere voṁdu sarvvanamaḥsivāyavāgi dēvara ·amrita-
paḍige ko-

ṭṭaru maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vvā yō harati vasuṁdharā[ṁ*] śaṣṭi-
rvaruṣasa-

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(A.R. No. 480 of 1915.)
ON A ROCK IN A FIELD AT SAMBUKALLU, ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is badly damaged. All that is preserved is the name of the Yādava king
Rāmachandra.

inscription.head:
No. 389.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1918.)
ON A BEAM IN THE MALLIKARJUNASVAMI TEMPLE AT KURUVATTI,
HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1104, Plava, Pushya, ba. 2, Su [kravāra*] corre-
sponding to A.D. 1181, December 25 Friday. It states that while Vikramāditya (II) of
the Gutta-vaṁśa was ruling over Beḷuhuge seventy and Beṇṇevūr twelve, his
Pradhāna, Susaṅga-Daṇṇāyaka, with the permission of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vijaya-
Pāṇḍyarasa, is said to have made, in the presence of Bācharasa and the Mahājanas
of Kuṟuvatti, a grant of 70 mattars of land for the service of the god Āhavamallēśvara
in addition to the vṛitti-land which had been formerly granted by Vikramāditya, the
elder. Lōkābharaṇa received the gift.

l.ka: dalu hiriya vikramāditya koṭṭa sthaḷavṛttisahita 70 mattaru bhūmiyaṁ śrīmadāhava-
mallēśvaradēvargge sarvvanamaśyavāgi śaka 1104 neya plavasaṁcharada puśya ba 3 śu

rvvāruvara(ṁ)numatiyiṁ sarvvanamaśyamene paḍeda lōkābharaṇaṁ [||*] pratipāḷakaṁgī
dharmma-vakku ||

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(A.R. No. 215-A of 1918.)
ON THE SAME BEAM.
inscription.p:
This is much mutilated and dated Śaka 1117 and states that Ballāḷa-Bhūpa,
probably the son-in-law of the Gutta chief Vikramāditya II, made some gift to
Lokābharaṇa-Munīndra for the service of the god Āhavamallēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 391.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1918.)
IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is not dated. The five hundred svāmis of Ayyāvoḷe, who are described as
setting the servants of Yama in motion, as roaring with the thunderbolt in hand, as
giving their enemies to Yama and as generous to the submissive, are said to have
gathered together with the Nānādēsi merchants and the Mummuridaṇḍas
in Kuṟuvatti and made a gift of one pāga of the hejjuṅka to Lōkābharaṇa-Munīndra
for the service of god Āhavamallesvara. As this Lōkābharaṇa is mentioned in No.
389, the present record may also belong to the time of Vikramāditya II.

l.ka:
ru muṁmuridaṁḍasāgaraṁgaḷuṁ kuṟuvattiyalli mahānāḍāgirddu || javadūtara jaḍḍaṁ
kūṟāḍuvarā baṟa-

siḍilaṁ piḍidu meṟevarpratiyappavanaṁ javaṁge kuḍuvarttavatavagōrorvvaroldaḍīvarkkā-
var || neredopputirddai-

nūrvvarāhumallēśvaraṁge pūjege lōkābharaṇamunigoldu hejjuṁkarāvuḷada pāgami-
ttarācaṁdrārkka ||

dēvarettu matte kōṇaṁ pēṟidaḍaṁ suṁkakāṇikeyuṁ koṁḍaṁ nāḍiṁge drōhiyendu
biṭṭaridaṁ pāḷipaṁge svargga ||

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(A.R. No. 379 of 1928.)
ON A STONE IN A FIELD CALLED POLALIMARU IN KARIYANGALA, NEAR
POLALI-AMMUNAJE, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and is not dated. It records (that the Āḷupa king) Raṇasāgara
made a gift to Nāgamma of Kayra-vaṁśa, who was as brave as Mahēndra, for
crushing the army of Pāybaya. The characters of the record belong to the 8th
century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(A.R. No. 416 of 1928.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF MAHALINGESVARA TEMPLE AT
BASRUR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1077, Bhāva, the month Dhanus and Saṅkrmaṇa. The
inscription belongs to the reign of the Āḷupa king Kaviyāḷupēndra. A certain
Maunayōgi is stated to have granted some land for the service of the god
Nakharēśvara of Basurepura.

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(A.R. No. 419 of 1928.)
ON ANOTHER SLAB IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This damaged record belongs to the reign of the Āḷupa king Pāṇḍyachakravarti
Kulaśēkharadēva and is dated the cyclic year Manmatha, Makara, 18, Sōmavāra
corresponding probably to A.D. 1176 January 12 Monday. It seems to record a
gift made by Chaṇḍabbe to the temple of the god Nakharēśvara at Basaṟūru.

l.note: The rest is damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE INNER PRAKARA OF THE KOTESVARA
TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, A SUBURB OF COONDAPUR, SAME TALUK AND
DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1183, Durmati, Mārgaśira, śu. 6 Maṅ[galavāra*] and Dhanur-
māsa 3, Jupiter in Makara, corresponding to A.D. 1261 November 27 Tuesday. It
refers itself to the joint rule of the senior queen Paṭṭamahādēvi and king Vīra-
Pāṇḍyadēv-Āḷpēndradēva and states that when Voḍḍama-Śrīdēva, Narasiṅgaverggaḍe,
other officers and priests had assembled, the three hundred of the town Kuḍikūru,
its Heggaḍe and Kōṭi-Mēlaṇṭa set up the inscription fixing the total revenue
of Kuḍikūr at gadyāṇas 180.

l.ka: māsa 3 neya maṁ daṁdu śrīmatu rā-
varige gaṁge vāraṇāsiya taḍiyalu sāyira kavi-
leya koṁda pāpa yī grāmavanitta pāpa yiṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(A.R. No. 364 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE PRAKARA OF THE KUNDESVARA TEMPLE AT
THE SAME VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
This is dated Śaka 1184, Dundubhi, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 13, Ādivāra corresponding to
A.D. 1262 August 13, Sunday in the reign of Paṭṭamahādēvi and king Pāṇḍyadēv-
Āḷpēndradēvarasa. Voḍḍamadēva, Narasiṅga-Heggaḍe and other officers are stated to
have fixed the revenue of Kundāpura at gadyāṇas 140. The king and Paṭṭamahādēvi
are said to have been ruling at Bārahakanyāpura.

l.note: The slab is built in after this.

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(A.R. No. 372 of 1927.)
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE NORTH VERANDA OF THE INNER PRAKARA IN THE
KOTESVARA TEMPLE AT KOTESVARA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is damaged and not dated. It refers to Pāṇḍyachakravarti Vīradēvāḷva
and registers a gift of paddy for the service of god Nārāyaṇadēva.

l.ka: daki mā 3 saluva krama || śrīmatu pa

l.note: Incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1928.)
ON A PIECE OF CARVED SLAB LYING NEAR THE DHVAJASTAMBHA OF DURGA-
PARAMESVARI TEMPLE AT POLALI AMMUNAJE, MANGALORE TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is not dated and much damaged. It belongs to the reign of Kula-
śē[khara] Pāṇḍyapaṭṭigadēva and mentions Bīravaḍi and Meḷavāḍi.

l.note: A few lines
Incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(A.R. No. 380 of 1928.)
ON A STONE SET UP IN A FIELD AT BADAKABAIL AT KARIANGALA,
SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This undated record belongs to the reign of Kumāra-Pāṇḍya Jayasiṅgarasa who
is stated here to have made a gift of the land called Paṇḍikaru to the goddess
Bhaṭṭāraki of Hoḷal.

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(A.R. No. 299 of 1905.)
ON ANOTHER SLAB SET UP IN A FIELD OF THE VILLAGE CHIPPILI, CLOSE TO THE
CHIPPILI-VIMPALLE ROAD, MADANAPALLE TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is not dated. It refers to the (Chōḻa) king Punyakomarān ruling over
Chirppuḷi, and records the death of a hero named Parasurāman in a battle. On
palaeographic grounds the record may be relegated to the 8th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(A.R. No. 792 of 1917.)
ON A BOULDER BY THE SIDE OF THE ROAD FROM BUDILI TO GORANTLA,
HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is not dated. It refers itself to the reign of the Chōḻa king Chōḻa-Mahārāja
and records that he made a grant of land, 12 khaṇḍugas (in sowing capacity),
and another gift to a temple.

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(A.R. No. 483 of 1906.)
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE BHIMESVARA TEMPLE AT CHAUDURU, PRODDATUR TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
The record is dated Śaka 1114, Parīdhāvi, Chaitra, śu. 1, Sōmavāra, corresponding
to A.D. 1192 March 16 Monday in the reign of the Chōḷa chief Na[la]siddaṇadēva-
Chōḷamahārāja ruling Rēnāḍu seventy, Kanne three-hundred and other districts from
Vallūrapura. He is stated to have levied tribute from Kāñchī. A certain Chōḷajīya-
Paṇḍita is stated to have granted some vṛitti-lands to Prōlajīya. The Sēnabōva
Mācharasa wrote and Bomōja engraved the inscription.

l.ka: rada caitra suddha pāḍiva sōmavāradaṁdu māca-
ra śrī • • • śvarada vṛttiyara prōlajīyaṁge dhā-
svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdha-

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(A.R. No. 831 of 1917.)
ON THE WALL OF THE DURGAPANCHA CAVE AT RAMATIRTHAM,
VIZIANAGARAM ZAMINDARI, VIZAGAPATAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This is the same as No. 372 of 1905 and belongs to the reign of the Eastern
Chāḷukya king Mummaḍi Bhīma, who also bore the title Vishṇuvardhana-Mahārāja.
The Jaina teacher named Trikālayōgi-Siddhāntadēva of the Dēsi-gaṇa and the teacher
of Vimalāditya, is said to have constructed the Rāmakoṇḍa. It is not dated.

l.ka:
svasti [||*] sarvvalōkāśraya śrīviṣṇuvardhanamahārāja rājamārttaṇḍa mummaḍibhīmana
gurugaḷ
